Pak Study MCQs · pages 501-550

Sharpen your Pak Study recall

Tap a card to reveal the explanation. Take it one seal at a time.

500 questions in this set
1

What is the purpose of the formation of all India National Congress?

Correct Answer: A platform for the civic and political dialogue among educated Indian
Show explanation
Explanation

The Indian National Congress was formed in 1885 with the primary purpose of providing a platform for civic and political dialogue among educated Indians.

The Congress was established by Allan Octavian Hume, a retired British Indian Civil Service officer, with the goal of creating a space where Indians could discuss and address political and social issues.

The Congress also aimed to promote Indian participation in the governance of their own country and to advocate for Indian rights and interests.

2

When Quetta destroyed by Earthquake in 1935?

Correct Answer: 31 May
Show explanation
Explanation
  • On 31 May 1936 Quetta was devasted by an earthquake;
  • ****
  • An earthquake occurred on 31 May 1935
  • An earthquake occurred between 2:33 am and 3:40 am at Quetta.
  • The causalities were up to 60,000
  • ***

Related Question to Earthquake:

  • Attabad lake emerged in the Hunza valley due to an Earthquake
  • A powerful earthquake killed at least 1000 people and left scores injured in Afghanistan in the early hours on 22 June 2022.
  • Who came to Pakistan after the earthquake as a special representative of UNO's earthquake relief? George Bush Sr.
  • Alaska U.S. state has had the most earthquakes
  • Which two countries witnessed a massive earthquake on October 30, 2020? Turkey and Greece
  • Which province was mostly hit by an earthquake in Afghanistan in June 2022? Paktika
  •  ***
  • An earthquake is a Natural Disaster
  • An activity related to an earthquake is Seismic activity
  • An earthquake can be simplified as a Shaking of the earth
    The intensity of an earthquake is measured by: the Richter scale
  • The earthquake had a magnitude of 7.7 Mw
  • ****
  • Richter Scale was developed in 1935.
  • Seismology is the study of earthquakes.
3

In which year Pakistan National Action Plan was established?

Correct Answer: December 25, 2014
Show explanation
Explanation

The National Action Plan (NAP) was created on December 25, 2014, in reaction to 133 children being murdered by the Taliban.

4

Pakistan is considered bridge between _______?

Correct Answer: All of the above
Show explanation
Explanation

Pakistan is strategically located at the crossroads of several regions, making it a bridge between:

  • Gulf and South Asia: Pakistan has historical and cultural ties with the Middle East and the Gulf region, and is also a part of South Asia.
  • Central Asia and South Asia: Pakistan shares borders with Central Asian countries like Afghanistan and has cultural and economic ties with the region, while also being a part of South Asia.
  • East Asia and South Asia: Pakistan has growing economic and trade ties with East Asian countries like China, and is also a part of South Asia.
5

اردو کلاسیکل اور جدید شاعری کا سنگم کس شاعر کو کہا جاتا ہے؟

Correct Answer: خواجہ الطاف حسین حالی.
Show explanation
Explanation

خواجہ الطاف حسین حالی کو اردو کلاسیکل اور جدید شاعری کا سنگم قرار دیا جاتا ہے۔

انہوں نے اپنی شاعری اور نثر کے ذریعے اردو ادب میں جدیدیت کا آغاز کیا،

6

Samjhauta express bomb blast occur in which year?

Correct Answer: February, 2007
Show explanation
Explanation

On 18 February 2007, 68 innocent passengers, including over 40 Pakistani nationals, lost their lives in a terrorist attack on Lahore-bound Samjhauta Express train.

The attack took place on India's soil.

The Samjhota Express was a train running between Delhi, India, and Lahore, Pakistan.

7

Which of the following act of East India Company provided a joint government of British India by the company and the crown with government holding the ultimate authority?

Correct Answer: Pitt's India act 1784
Show explanation
Explanation

Pitt's India Act of 1784 provided for a joint government of British India by the East India Company and the Crown, with the government holding the ultimate authority.

This act established a Board of Control, which consisted of six members, including a Secretary of State and the Chancellor of the Exchequer.

The act also reduced the Governor-General's council to three members and gave the Governor-General the final say.

The Presidencies of Madras and Bombay were made subordinate to the Bengal Presidency.

Calcutta became the capital of the British possessions in India.

8

When was Lucknow recaptured by British Forces?

Correct Answer: 1858
Show explanation
Explanation

Lucknow was recaptured by British forces in 1858, during the Indian Rebellion of 1857, also known as the Sepoy Mutiny.

The rebellion began in May 1857, and Lucknow, the capital of the Awadh region.

It was a key center of resistance against British rule.

The British forces, led by General Sir Colin Campbell, recaptured Lucknow in March 1858, after a prolonged siege and fierce fighting.

9

Pervez Musharraf returned from which country when he imposed Martial Law?

Correct Answer: Sri Lanka
Show explanation
Explanation

Pervez Musharraf returned from Sri Lanka when he imposed martial law in Pakistan on October 12, 1999.

He was on an official visit to Sri Lanka and was dismissed as military chief while he was away.

He returned to Pakistan and led a military coup against the government, eventually declaring himself the chief executive of the country.

10

A glacier ______?

Correct Answer: Is a river of ice sliding down a mountain carrying along material
Show explanation
Explanation

A glacier is a river of ice sliding down a mountain carrying along with morainic material.

Glaciers usually deform and get down their weight.

On earth, 99% of glacial ice is there in vast ice sheets in the polar regions and the mountain ranges.


11

Which province of Pakistan has the most canals?

Correct Answer: Punjab
Show explanation
Explanation

Punjab province has the most canals in Pakistan, with an extensive network of irrigation canals that support agriculture in the region.

The province is often referred to as the "breadbasket" of Pakistan due to its fertile soil and extensive irrigation system.


Some of the major canals in Punjab include:

  • Upper Bari Doab Canal
  • Lower Bari Doab Canal
  • Upper Chenab Canal
  • Lower Chenab Canal
  • Ravi Canal
  • Sutlej Canal
12

First International Flight of PIA was taken on ____?

Correct Answer: 1 February 1955
Show explanation
Explanation

Pakistan International Airlines (PIA) took its first international flight on February 1, 1955


It is the flag carrier of Pakistan.


Headquarter: Karachi


First name: Orient airways

13

If a contesting candidate death after nominations election will be

Correct Answer: Cancelled
Show explanation
Explanation

If a contesting candidate dies after the nomination process,

The election will typically be canceled and re-scheduled, depending on the election rules and the jurisdiction.

14

Which Article is related to the Objective Resolution?

Correct Answer: Article 2A
Show explanation
Explanation

The resolution, in its entirety, has been made part of the Constitution of Pakistan under Article 2(A).

The Constituent Assemb Pakistan adopted the Objectives Resolution on March 12, 1949.

Prime Minister, Liaquat Ali Khan, had presented it in the assembly on March 7, 1949

Out of 75 members of the assembly, 21 voted for it.

All the amendments proposed by minority members were reject

Consequently, all ten of them voted against it.


15

Election rules are made in:

Correct Answer: Article 227
Show explanation
Explanation

Article 227 of the Constitution of Pakistan provides the framework for making rules regarding elections and ensures that the laws are consistent with the Constitution.

16

The enlistment of political parties is mentioned in which article?

Correct Answer: Article 219
Show explanation
Explanation

Article 219 provides for the registration and enlistment of political parties with the Election Commission of Pakistan, ensuring transparency and accountability.

17

The requirement for 5% women participation in political activities is mentioned in:

Correct Answer: Section 206
Show explanation
Explanation

HRCP echoes the Aurat Foundation's concern that the main political parties have failed to ensure that at least 5 percent of their party tickets for general seats are awarded to women per Section 206 of the Elections Act 2017.

18

The article related to adjourned elections is:

Correct Answer: Article 224
Show explanation
Explanation

Article 224 deals with the circumstances under which elections may be adjourned, including the process and authority for rescheduling.

19

In the case of a candidate's death, the election process is governed by:

Correct Answer: Article 232
Show explanation
Explanation

 Article 232 outlines the procedures to be followed if a candidate dies before the election, including the holding of elections for the vacated seat.

20

The allocation of political symbols is mentioned in which article?

Correct Answer: Article 216
Show explanation
Explanation

Article 216 governs the allocation of electoral symbols to political parties and independent candidates, ensuring each party has a unique identifier on the ballot.

21

The article related to election expenses is:

Correct Answer: Article 207
Show explanation
Explanation

Article 207 sets the limits and regulations concerning the expenses that candidates can incur during the election campaign.

22

The Election Commission of Pakistan (ECP) can declare an election void under which section?

Correct Answer: Section 90
Show explanation
Explanation

 Section 90 of the Election Act empowers the ECP to declare an election void 

If there are substantial irregularities or violations of the election laws

23

Who is authorized to print ballot papers?

Correct Answer: Election Commission of Pakistan
Show explanation
Explanation

The Election Commission of Pakistan is responsible for the printing of ballot papers, ensuring security and integrity throughout the election process.

24

When did Pakistan declare itself a nuclear state?

Correct Answer: May 28, 1998
Show explanation
Explanation

Pakistan conducted a series of nuclear tests on 28 May 1998 at the Chagai test site, in Balochistan.

5 Nuclear tests were conducted by Pakistan at 15:15 hrs PST on 28 May 1998.

The tests were performed at Ras Koh Hills in the Chagai District of Balochistan Province.

Chagai-I was Pakistan's first public test of nuclear weapons.

while 

Chagai II

Chagai-II is the codename assigned to the second atomic test conducted by Pakistan, carried out on 30 May 1998

These tests followed the Chagai-I series

The test site was Kharan Desert, Pakistan

The number of tests is 1

25

NFC was established under which article of the Constitution of Pakistan?

Correct Answer: Article 160
Show explanation
Explanation

The National Finance Commission (NFC) is a constitutional body in Pakistan that was created under Article 160 of the Constitution of 1973  

It makes recommendations for the financial allocation.

26

When was the Constitution Revival Order made?

Correct Answer: 1985
Show explanation
Explanation

1973 Constitution is restored in amended form by General Ziaul Haq through the 'Revival of the 1973 Constitution Order, 1985'.

27

Dogra dynasty bought Kashmir from the British in the year?

Correct Answer: 1846
Show explanation
Explanation

The Dogra dynasty was a dynasty of Hindu Rajputs who ruled Jammu & Kashmir from 1846 to 1947.

After the First Anglo-Sikh War in 1846, the British gave Kashmir and the title of 'Maharaja' to Gulab Singh


AGM 30.4.23

مثنوی "مرغ نامہ" کا مصنف کون ہے؟

Explanation

مثنوی "مرغ نامہ" کے مصنف عبدالرحمٰن خان مجبور ہیں۔

یہ مثنوی پہلی بار 1864ء میں شائع ہوئی۔







29

Which province of Pakistan has the most divisions?

Correct Answer: Punjab
Show explanation
Explanation

 Punjab is the most populous province of Pakistan and has the highest number of divisions,

which facilitates administrative management and governance. As of now, Punjab has 10 divisions.

Verified on 28-09-2024

30

In which province of Pakistan is the Big Canal System primarily located?

Correct Answer: Punjab
Show explanation
Explanation
 The Big Canal System, also known as the Indus Canal System, is mainly situated in Punjab, playing a vital role in irrigation.
 The Big Canal System utilizes the waters of the Indus River and its tributaries for irrigation.
Which of the following components of the Big Canal System?
A) Bhakra Canal
B) Lower Chenab Canal
C) Upper Chenab Canal
31

Who is the author of "Maddo Jazar-e-Islaam"?

Correct Answer: Maulana Altaf Hussain Hali
Show explanation
Explanation

Maulana Altaf Hussain Hali is famous for his Musaddas-e-Madd-o-Jazr-e-Islam, 

The poem was published in 1879 in Tahzīb ul-Akhlāq and in book form

32

The book "Dasht-e-Qais Laila" was written by:

Correct Answer: Kishwar Naweed
Show explanation
Explanation

"Dasht-e-Qais Laila" is a notable literary work by Kishwar Naheed.

It was first published in 1971.

33

Subtropical temperatures in Pakistan ______ in summer?

Correct Answer: Rise
Show explanation
Explanation

Subtropical temperatures in Pakistan rise in summer.


This is because Pakistan experiences high temperatures during the summer

Due to its subtropical climate, especially in regions like Punjab, Sindh, and parts of Balochistan, temperatures can soar significantly.

34

Where was Imam Malik born?

Correct Answer: Medina
Show explanation
Explanation

Imam Malik Born: 711 AD, Medina, Saudi Arabia

He was one of the most respected scholars of FIQH

His full name is Malik bin Anas bin Malik.

Imam-e-Dar-ul-Hijrat was the title of Imam Malik

Imam Malik Died: 795 AD, Medina, Saudi Arabia




35

Who was the Bengali leader who served as the Prime Minister of Pakistan?

Correct Answer: Khawaja Nazimuddin
Show explanation
Explanation

Khawaja Nazimuddin and Huseyn Shaheed Suhrawardy both were Pakistani Bengali barrister and politician.

Khawaja Nazimuddin was the second prime minister of Pakistan from 1951 to 1953.

Huseyn Shaheed Suhrawardy served as 5th Prime Minister of Pakistan from 12 September 1956 – 17 October 1957



36

National Fish of Pakistan?

Correct Answer: See the answer to this question in the explanation section
Show explanation
Explanation
  • The National Fish of Pakistan is Mahseer
  • Mahseer fish are found in every province throughout Pakistan in semi-cold fresh water.
  • It is also known as the “King of Swat”.
37

According to the 1956 constitution, President of Pakistan should be ____?

Correct Answer: Muslim
Show explanation
Explanation

According to the 1956 constitution of Pakistan, the President should be a Muslim۔

Constitution of 1956 · Pakistan was declared as the Islamic Republic

while

Both Urdu and Bengali were declared as official and national languages of Pakistan in the 1956 constitution

              All important questions of  "the 1956 constitution"

              1956 constitution is promulgated on 23 March 1956

              ***

              Iskander Mirza abrogated the 1956 constitution

              ***

              In 1956 the designation of Governor General changed to President

              ****

              40 years was the age prescribed for president in the 1956 constitution

              **

              The Constitution of 1956 contained 234 articles divided into thirteen parts and six schedules

              **

              The constitution of 1956 remained implemented for 2 years 6 months and 14 days.

              **

              The constitution of 1956 provided a Parliamentary form of government for the country.

              **

              1956 constitution legislature was Unicameral

              **

              The Constitution of 1956 provided for a federal system with the principle of parity between East Pakistan and West Pakistan

              ND16-1-2023

              38

              China Pakistan Economic Corridor will be fully operational by _____?

              Correct Answer: 2030
              Show explanation
              Explanation

              The China-Pakistan Economic Corridor (CPEC) is expected to be fully operational by 2030.

              The China-Pakistan Economic Corridor (CPEC) is a series of energy and infrastructure projects, some of which connect China’s Xinjiang to Gwadar.

              Initiated in mid-2013 and formally launched in April 20, 2015.

              CPEC is generally described as a $46 billion package of Chinese aid.

              39

              Most glaciers after Arctic region are located in _____?

              Correct Answer: Pakistan
              Show explanation
              Explanation

              After the Arctic, Pakistan has the largest number of glaciers outside the polar regions.

              Major glaciers include Siachen, Baltoro, Biafo, and Hispar, mostly in the Gilgit-Baltistan region.

              40

              When Pakistan joined World Trade Organization (WTO)?

              Correct Answer: 1 January 1995
              Show explanation
              Explanation
              • Pakistan joined WTO: January 1, 1995
              • WTO: World Trade Organization
              • Members: 164
              • Headquarters: Geneva, Switzerland
              • Created: January 1, 1995
              • Official languages: English, French, Spanish
              • Successor of: General Agreement on Tariffs and Trade (GATT)
              41

              The Edhi Foundation has the world's largest ____?

              Correct Answer: Ambulance Service
              Show explanation
              Explanation

              The Edhi Foundation is a non-profit social welfare organization based in Pakistan.

              It was founded by Abdul Sattar Edhi in 1951.

              Headquarters of Edhi Foundation located in Karachi.

              42

              Lala Lajpat Rai was a famous Punjabi leader. He was associated with _______?

              Correct Answer: Arya Samaj
              Show explanation
              Explanation

              Lajpat Rai was a heavyweight veteran leader of the Indian Nationalist Movement.

              He was led by the Indian National Congress, Hindu reform movements and Arya Samaj.

              He was the founder of Arya Samaj.

              He died on November 17, 1928

              43

              Partition of Bangal was annulled under British Monarch?

              Correct Answer: King George V
              Show explanation
              Explanation

              Ø  On the visiting His Majesty King George V to Indo-Pakistan and holding of Darbar at Delhi on 12th December 1911 the partition of Bengal was cancelled.

                  ****

              Ø  Partition of Bengal Announced on 19 July 1905 by Lord Curzon for Administrative requirements

              Ø  Congress protest against Partition of Bengal


              44

              Who was the first Pakistani doctor who lost his life due to COVID-19 infection and was posthumously awarded Nishan-e-Kashmir on March 27, 2020?

              Correct Answer: Usama Riaz
              Show explanation
              Explanation

              Riaz died on 23 March 2020, from COVID-19 complications at the age of 26.

              Riaz was the first Pakistani doctor to die from the virus.

              On 27 March 2020, he was posthumously awarded the Nishan-e-Kashmir, the highest award of the state, by Prime Minister of Azad Kashmir for his services.

              45

              Due to Geostrategic position, no peace is possible in Afghanistan without the active support and cooperation of ______?

              Correct Answer: Pakistan
              Show explanation
              Explanation

              Pakistan shares a long border with Afghanistan and has significant geopolitical influence in the region. 

              Due to its strategic location, Pakistan plays a crucial role in Afghanistan's stability and security.

              Historically, Pakistan has had close ties with various Afghan groups and has been involved in Afghan affairs.

              46

              Gulbadan Begum was Emperor Humayun's _____?

              Correct Answer: Sister
              Show explanation
              Explanation

              Gulbadan Begum Daughter of Emperor Babur.

              She is the author of Humayun-Nama about the life of her half-brother, Emperor Humayun.

              47

              In the context of Pakistan's political and judicial history what does PCO stands for?

              Correct Answer: Provincial Constitutional Order
              Show explanation
              Explanation

              In Pakistan's political and judicial history, PCO stands for Provincial Constitutional Order.

              It issued during military regimes to suspend or amend the constitution, often affecting the judiciary.

              48

              Which article of the constitution of Pakistan is relevant to the national language?

              Correct Answer: Article 251
              Show explanation
              Explanation

              According to Article 251 of the Constitution of Pakistan, Urdu is declared as the national language of Pakistan.

              This article emphasizes the importance of promoting and developing Urdu as the lingua franca of the country.

              It also allowing provincial languages to be used at the provincial level.

              49

              The latest anti-militancy military operation in Pakistan is titled?

              Correct Answer: Radd-ul-Fasaad
              Show explanation
              Explanation

              Radd-ul-Fasaad is a military operation.

              It was start by COAS Qamar Javed Bajwa across all states of Pakistan, on 22 February 2017. 

              50

              Mr. Javed Iqbal was Chairman of the _____?

              Correct Answer: National Accountability Bureau
              Show explanation
              Explanation

              Mr. Javed Iqbal was the Chairman of the National Accountability Bureau (NAB) of Pakistan.

              He served in this position from October 11, 2017, to June 3, 2022.

              He is also a retired Senior Justice of the Supreme Court of Pakistan.

              51

              The Lucknow pact granted the right of Separate Electorates to the _____?

              Correct Answer: Muslims
              Show explanation
              Explanation

              On 1st October 1906, a Muslim political delegation led by Aga Khan


              Met with the Viceroy of India to demand a separate electorate for Muslims.


              This delegation played a crucial role in the establishment of the Muslim League in 1906

              52

              Which street still reminds the people of Lahore of the great daily of Munshi Mehboob Alam?

              Correct Answer: Paisa Akhbar street
              Show explanation
              Explanation

              Paisa Akhbar street in Lahore still reminds people of the great daily, Munshi Mehboob Alam.

              Paisa Akhbar was a popular Urdu-language newspaper founded by Munshi Mehboob Alam in 1891.

              53

              Forex stands for _____?

              Correct Answer: For exchange
              Show explanation
              Explanation

              Forex stands for Foreign Exchange.

              It refers to the global market where individuals, businesses, and institutions trade currencies.

              It's also known as the FX market.

              54

              Tobacco was introduced for cultivation during the reign of _____?

              Correct Answer: Jahangir
              Show explanation
              Explanation

              Tobacco was introduced to India during the reign of Jahangir (1605-1627), the fourth Mughal Emperor.

              Jahangir was fond of tobacco and encouraged its cultivation and trade in India.

              He even granted a monopoly on the tobacco trade to the English East India Company, which helped to establish tobacco as a major cash crop in India.

              55

              The tomb of Hazrat Madhu Lal Hussain is in _____?

              Correct Answer: Lahore
              Show explanation
              Explanation

              Hazrat Madho Lal Hussain

              • Born: 1539 CE (945 AH), Lahore, Punjab
              • Died: 1599 CE (1008 AH)
              • Shrine: G.T. Road, Lahore, near UET Lahore

              Area wise second largest province of Pakistan is?

              Correct Answer: Punjab
              Show explanation
              Explanation

              List of largest Province of Pakistan


              Sr. No. Province / Capital Territory Area (km²)
              1 Balochistan 347,190
              2 Punjab 205,345
              3 Sindh 140,914
              4 KPK 101,741
              57

              Which dynasty ruled after Slave Dynasty?

              Correct Answer: Khilji
              Show explanation
              Explanation

              The Khilji dynasty ruled India after the Slave Dynasty (also known as the Mamluk Dynasty).

              The Slave Dynasty was overthrown by Jalaluddin Firuz Khilji in 1290.

              This marking the beginning of the Khilji dynasty, which ruled until 1320.


              Additional Information:

              • Slave Dynasty (1206-1290)
              • Khilji Dynasty (1290-1320)
              • Tughleq Dynasty (1320-1414)
              • Sayyid Dynasty (1414-1451)
              • Lodhi Dynasty (1451-1526)
              58

              Who was the Mughal emperor before Aurangzeb?

              Correct Answer: Shah Jahan
              Show explanation
              Explanation

              Before Aurangzeb, Shah Jahan was the Mughal emperor.

              He is known for his architectural achievements.

              Including the construction of the Taj Mahal, during his reign from 1628 to 1658.


              Additional Information:

              • Babur (1526–1530)
              • Humayun (1530–1556)
              • Akbar (1556–1605)
              • Jahangir (1605–1627)
              • Shah Jahan (1627–1658)
              • Aurangzeb (1658–1707)
              59

              Which one was signed on 8 April 1950?

              Correct Answer: Liaqat Nehru Pact
              Show explanation
              Explanation

              The Nehru-Liaquat Pact, also known as the Delhi Pact

              It was a bilateral treaty between India and Pakistan

              It was signed on 08-April-1950.

              Expiration: 8 April 1956

              Signatories:

              • Jawahar Lal Nehru(Prime Minister of India)
              •  Liaquat Ali Khan (Prime Minister of Pakistan)
              60

              Which is the authorized agency to sanction licenses for cable television and FM radio?

              Correct Answer: PEMRA
              Show explanation
              Explanation

              PEMRA (Pakistan Electronic Media Regulatory Authority) is the authorized agency to sanction licenses for cable television and FM radio in Pakistan.

              PEMRA was established in 2002 to regulate and monitor electronic media in Pakistan, including television, radio, and digital media.

              PEMRA's responsibilities include:

              • Issuing licenses for cable television and FM radio
              • Regulating content and advertising on electronic media
              • Ensuring compliance with media regulations and codes of conduct
              • Protecting consumer rights and interests
              61

              Nusrat Fateh Ali Khan was died in which year?

              Correct Answer: 1997
              Show explanation
              Explanation

              Nusrat Fateh Ali Khan was a legendary Pakistani Qawwali singer known globally for his contributions to Sufi music.

              Born: October 13, 1948

              Died: August 16, 1997, in London, UK, at the age of 48.

              62

              FPSC Act was formed under which act of India?

              Correct Answer: 1935
              Show explanation
              Explanation

              Pakistan established the Federal Public Service Commission (FPSC) in 1947 under the provisions of the Government of India Act of 1935.

              The FPSC currently operates under the FPSC Ordinance of 1977.

              The 1935 Act established a Public Service Commission for each province.

              It also introduced provincial autonomy and federal features in the legislature.

              63

              Which of the following was inaugurated on 1 July 1948?

              Correct Answer: SBP
              Show explanation
              Explanation

              Quaid-e-Azam Muhammad Ali Jinnah inaugurated  State Bank of Pakistan on 1st July 1948, aspired to build the economic and financial system of the country in line with injunctions of Islam.

              Zahid Hussain the First Governor of the State Bank of Pakistan


              64

              Deen-e-Illahi was abolished by which Mughal King?

              Correct Answer: Aurangzeb
              Show explanation
              Explanation

              Aurangzeb abolished Deen-e-Illahi, the syncretic religion founded by Akbar, and enforced strict Islamic law (Sharia) in its place.


              The Dīn-i-Ilāh has known during its time as Tawḥīd-i-Ilāhī

              The name Dīn-i Ilāhī literally translates to "God's Religion", "Religion of God"

              Mughal emperor Akbar enforced Din-I-Ilahi in 1582

              According to the renowned historian Mubarak Ali, Dīn-i Ilāhī is a name that was not used in Akbar's period.

              At the time, it was called Tawhid-i-Ilāhī ("Divine Monotheism"), as it is written by Abu'l-Fazl, a court historian during the reign of Akbar

              Type: Syncretic religion

              Region: Indian subcontinent Fatehpur Sikri, Agra, Mughal Empire.


              65

              The boundary dispute between Pakistan and China was settled during the government of:

              Correct Answer: Ayoub Khan
              Show explanation
              Explanation


              A boundary agreement was signed between Pakistan and China in 2 March 1963.

              Negotiations between the nations officially began on October 13, 1962,

              It was signed by:

              • Foreign Minister Chen Yi for the Chinese
              • Zulfikar Ali Bhutto for the Pakistani

              The eastern tip of the Wakhan Corridor starts the Sino-Pak border between the People's Republic of China and Pakistan.

              66

              The first Sikh War in 1845 was fought between Sikhs and:

              Correct Answer: British
              Show explanation
              Explanation

              The first Anglo-Sikh war was fought between the Sikh Empire and the British East India Company

              Fought in 1845 and 1846

              around the Ferozepur district of Punjab

              Lord Harding was the Governor General during the First Anglo-Sikh War.


              Additional information:

              The second Anglo-Sikh war took place in 1848-1849.

              The Governor General of the Second Anglo-Sikh War was Lord Dalhousie.



              67

              General Yahya Khan became the Commander-in-Chief (C-in-C) of the Pakistan Army in 1966. Who was C-in-C before him?

              Correct Answer: General Musa Khan
              Show explanation
              Explanation
              General Sir Frank Messervy was appointed as the first Army Commander-in-Chief of the new Pakistan Army.

              General Messervy was succeeded in this post in February 1948, by General Sir Douglas Gracey, who served until January 1951.
              LIST OF Army Commander-in-Chief

              S.No 

              Name

              Took Office

              Left Office

              Duration

              1

              General

              Sir Frank Walter Messervy KCSI, KBE, CB, DSO & Bar

              15 August 1947

              68

              One of the founders of All India Muslim League, Salimullah Khan, was the Nawab of ____?

              Correct Answer: Dhaka
              Show explanation
              Explanation

              Nawab Salimullah, the 3rd Nawab of Dhaka and founder of All India Muslim League.

              He was the founder President of Bengal Muslim League in 1907.

              The first Nawab of Dhaka was Khwaja Hafizullah.

              The the second Nawab of Dhaka was Khwaja Ahsanullah.

              69

              In Muslim scientist who is known as The Father of Chemistry?

              Correct Answer: see the answer
              Show explanation
              Explanation

              The Muslim scientist known as the "Father of Chemistry" is Jabir ibn Hayyan,

              70

              Of the neighbouring districts closest to Lahore city is _____?

              Correct Answer: Sheikhupura
              Show explanation
              Explanation

              Sheikhupura is the neighboring district closest to Lahore city.

              It is located approximately 38 kilometers northwest of Lahore.

              Sheikhupura is often considered a suburb of Lahore due to its proximity.

              It is part of the Lahore Metropolitan Area.

              71

              The first Emperor of Tughluq Dynasty?

              Correct Answer: Ghiyasuddin Tughlaq
              Show explanation
              Explanation

              Ghiyath al-Din Tughluq, was the founder of the Tughluq dynasty in India.

              who reigned over the Sultanate of Delhi from 1320 to 1325.

              He founded the city of Tughluqabad.

              72

              Rann of Kutch is situated in ____?

              Correct Answer: South of Tharparkar
              Show explanation
              Explanation

              The Rann of Kutch is a large salt marsh located to the south of Tharparkar in the Sindh region of Pakistan.

              It extending into the Indian state of Gujarat.

              73

              In Punjab Radcliffe gave an unjust award, including the following tehsils on the Indian side?

              Correct Answer: Gurdaspur, Pathankot
              Show explanation
              Explanation

              The Punjab Boundary Commission Award (known as the Radcliffe. Award)

              In addition to Gurdaspur's Muslim majority tehsils, Radcliffe also gave the Muslim majority tehsils of:

              Ajnala (Amritsar District),

              Zira,

              Firozpur (in Firozpur District),

              Nakodar

              Jullander (in Jullander District) to India instead of Pakistan

              ND26-2-2023

              74

              Syed Ahmad Shaheed was the founder of which of the following movement?

              Correct Answer: Jihad
              Show explanation
              Explanation

              Syed Ahmad Shaheed (1786-1831) was a Muslim leader and reformer who founded the Jihad movement in India.

              He led a rebellion against the Sikh Empire in the early 19th century.

              The Jihad movement, also known as the Tariqah-i-Muhammadiyah.

              It aimed to reform Islamic practices, promote religious purification, and resist British colonial rule.

              75

              When was Pakistan Steel Mill in Karachi established?

              Correct Answer: July 2, 1973
              Show explanation
              Explanation

              Pakistan Steel Mill in Karachi was founded on July 2, 1973.

              PM Zulfikar Ali Bhutto laid the foundation stone of Pakistan Steel Mills on December 30, 1973, with the support of the Soviet Union (Russia).

              An agreement for techno-financial assistance was made between Pakistan and the Soviet Union in 1971.

              It is the country's largest industrial undertaking.

              It has a production capacity of 1.1 million tons of steel.

              It expandable up to 3.0 million tons per annum.

              76

              Which of the following Pakistani player won the 1994 World Snooker Championship?

              Correct Answer: Muhammad Yousuf
              Show explanation
              Explanation

              The Pakistani player who won the 1994 World Snooker Championship is Muhammad Yousaf.

              He defeated Johannes R. Johanneson of Iceland 11-9 in Johannesburg to claim the championship.

              In 1994 Pakistan was world champion of:

              1.      Cricket
              2.      Hockey
              3.      Squash
              4.      Snooker
              77

              At the NAM Summit in Durban (1998), which issue was discussed concerning Pakistan?

              Correct Answer: Kashmir Problem
              Show explanation
              Explanation

              At the NAM Summit in Durban in 1998, the Kashmir issue was discussed concerning Pakistan.

              Pakistan raised the matter to garner international support for resolving the dispute with India.

              The Non-Aligned Movement (NAM) is a forum of 120 developing world states.
              78

              Mohsin Hamid is famous for _____?

              Correct Answer: English Writing
              Show explanation
              Explanation

              Mohsin Hamid is a famous English-language novelist.

              It is known for works like "The Reluctant Fundamentalist" and "Exit West."

              His novels often explore themes of identity and globalization.

              79

              Famous cooking expert and T.V. personality Zubaida Tariq passed away on ____?

              Correct Answer: 4th January 2018
              Show explanation
              Explanation

              Zubaida Tariq age: 75 years

              Born: 4 April 1942

              Died: 4 January 2018

              Burial place: Karachi, Pakistan

              Other names: Zubaida Aapa

              80

              When Lahore Declaration between the Prime Ministers of Pakistan and India was signed?

              Correct Answer: 1999
              Show explanation
              Explanation
              • The Lahore Declaration, signed on February 21, 1999.
              • It was signed between Prime Ministers Nawaz Sharif of Pakistan and Atal Bihari Vajpayee of India.
              • The treaty aimed to reduce nuclear risks and build confidence between the two nations.

              Last verified on 30-05-2026

              81

              Name the first Pakistani female Cricketer to take 100 ODI wickets?

              Correct Answer: Sana Mir
              Show explanation
              Explanation

              Sana Mir is the first Pakistani female cricketer to take 100 ODI wickets.

              Nida Dar is the first Pakistani female cricketer to take 100 T20I wickets.

              82

              Harappa is a city in Punjab about 24 km South west of _____?

              Correct Answer: Sahiwal
              Show explanation
              Explanation

              Harappa is an ancient city located about 24 km southwest of Sahiwal in Punjab, Pakistan.

              Harappa is situated along the banks of the Ravi River.

              It is located in the Sahiwal district Punjab province of Pakistan.

              The Harappan Civilization is also called as Indus Valley Civilization.

              83

              All India Muslim League was established in which city?

              Correct Answer: Dhaka
              Show explanation
              Explanation

              All India Muslim League Founded on 30 December 1906 at Dhaka, Bangladesh


              It was Founder by Nawab Khwaja Salimullah

              Its Headquarters is located in Lucknow

              They were Dissolved on 14 August 1947

              1st President of All India Muslim League is Sir Agha Khan



              جب 1947 میں مسلم لیگ ختم ہوئی تو یہ تین پارٹیاں بن گئی

              1- Muslim League in Pakistan

              2- Awami League in Bangladesh

              3- Indian Union Muslim League in India

              84

              The Act which is base of current Acts of India and Pakistan?

              Correct Answer: Government of India Act 1935
              Show explanation
              Explanation
              • The Government of India Act 1935 was a comprehensive legislation passed by the British Parliament.
              • That served as the foundation for the current legal framework in India and Pakistan.

              Correct Answer: Nasirul Mulk
              Show explanation

              List of Caretaker Prime Ministers of Pakistan

               

              Number   

              Name

              Duration

              1

              Ghulam Mustafa Jatoi

              6 Aug 1990 to 6 Nov 1990

              2

              Balakh Sher Mazari

              18 Apr 1993 to 26 May 1993

              3

              Moeenuddin Qureshi

              18 July to 19 October 1993

              4

              Malik Meraj Khalid

              6 Nov 1996 to 17 Feb 1997

              16 Nov 2007 to 24 Mar 2008

              86

              Viceroy term is made for used in which years?

              Correct Answer: 1858
              Show explanation
              Explanation

              Lord Canning was the first viceroy of India.

              Lord Mountbatten was the last viceroy of India.

              *********

              •Governor-General of Bengal (1773-1833):
              •When East India Company came to India, it controlled Bengal through a post named as "Governor of Bengal".
              •First Governor of Bengal: Robert Clive.
              •After the passing of Regulating Act 1773, the post of Governor of Bengal was converted into "Governor-General of Bengal"
              •First Governor-General of Bengal was Warren Hastings.
              •Governor-General of India (1833-58): Charter Act of 1833, the post name of Governor-General of Bengal again converted into "Governor-General of India".
              •First Governor-General of India was William Bentinck.
              •Viceroy (1858-1947):
              •After the revolt of 1857, the company rule was abolished and India came under the direct control of the British crown.
              •Government of India Act 1858 passed which changed the name of post Governor General of India by Viceroy of India.
              •The Viceroy was appointed directly by the British government.

              •The first Viceroy of India was Lord Canning.
              87

              Muhammad Ali Bogra was succeeded by?

              Correct Answer: Chaudhary Muhammad Ali
              Show explanation
              Explanation

              Chaudhary Muhammad Ali took over as the Prime Minister of Pakistan after Muhammad Ali Bogra resigned in 1955.

              Chaudhary Muhammad Ali served as the Prime Minister from 1955 to 1956.

              88

              Major Shabbir Sharif was awarded with Nishan-e-Haider posthumously. Which other medal for bravery was he awarded during his career in the Army?

              Correct Answer: Sitara-e-Jurat
              Show explanation
              Explanation

              Major Shabbir Sharif  is the only recipient to have ever received both:

              • Nishan-e-Haider (NH): Awarded posthumously for the 1971 war.
              • Sitara-e-Jurat (SJ): Awarded earlier for actions during the 1965 war.


              He was commissioned in Pakistan Army on 19 April 1961


              Born on April 28, 1943 in Kunjah, Gujarat.


              Shabbir Sharif sacrificed his life on December 6, 1971.

              89

              PAEC is the abbreviation of ____?

              Correct Answer: Pakistan Atomic Energy Commission
              Show explanation
              Explanation
              As the Energy Council Act went into full effect, Prime Minister Huseyn Suhrawardy established the Pakistan Atomic Energy Commission (PAEC) in March 1956.
              Its first chair was Nazir Ahmad – an experimental physicist.
              90

              The Military Academy Kakul is situated in _____?

              Correct Answer: Abbottabad
              Show explanation
              Explanation

              Pakistan Military Academy is located near Kakul Village in the City and District Abbottabad, Khyber Pakhtunkhwa

              Established on 14 October 1947

              The academy consists of four training battalions and 16 companies


              91

              Parliament of Pakistan means?

              Correct Answer: National Assembly and Senate
              Show explanation
              Explanation
              • The Upper House of Parliament of Pakistan is Senate.
              • The Lower House of Parliament of Pakistan is National Assembly.
              • The Parliament of Pakistan is bicameral.
              • Article 50 of the Constitution states that the Parliament of Pakistan consists of the President and Prime Minister.
              • Two Houses are known as:
                •             The National Assembly (Lower House)
                •             The Senate (Upper House)
              92

              Arshad Nadeem of Pakistan won the Olympic gold medal in which sports?

              Correct Answer: Javelin throw
              Show explanation
              Explanation

              Arshad Nadeem set a new Olympic record by clinching the coveted gold medal in the men’s javelin final

              This ended Pakistan’s 32-year wait for success at the Games 

              Throw: 92.97-m throw

              Winning date: 8 Aug 2024

              Arshad belongs to Mian Channu.

               Nadeem’s throw was Pakistan’s first individual gold medal

              Note: It also shattered the previous Olympic record of 90.57m, set by the Netherlands’ Andreas Thorkildsen at the Beijing 2008 Olympics.

              The throw now stands as the sixth longest throw ever, and the best in the world this year.

               ****

              93

              Who became the first Pakistani woman nominated to the ICC international Panel of Development Umpires?

              Correct Answer: Saleema Imtiaz
              Show explanation
              Explanation

              Saleema Imtiaz made history by becoming the first Pakistani woman to be nominated to the ICC International Panel of Development Umpires.

              This prestigious appointment qualifies Imtiaz to officiate in international women’s bilateral matches and ICC Women’s Events, marking a historic moment for the sport in Pakistan.

              ICC: International Cricket Council 

              ****

              94

              Sindh was defeated by the British in which battle?

              Correct Answer: Battle of Miani
              Show explanation
              Explanation

              The British conquered Sindh in 1843 after their victory in the Battle of Miani.

              The annexation of Sindh to British India was formalized through the signing of the Treaty of Annexation in 1847.

              95

              Abbreviation: PPL

              Correct Answer: Pakistan Petroleum Limited
              Show explanation
              Explanation

              Pakistan Petroleum Limited (PPL)

              It is a Pakistani state-owned oil and gas company headquartered in Karachi

              Headquarters: Karachi

              Founded: June 5, 1950

              The Suri Dynasty of India lasted for _____?

              Correct Answer: 15 years
              Show explanation
              Explanation

              The Suri Dynasty, founded by Sher Shah Suri.

              He ruled India from 1540 to 1555.

              He was lasting for 15 years before the Mughal Empire was restored by Humayun.

              98

              After Iran, which is the largest manufacturer of carpets?

              Correct Answer: Pakistan
              Show explanation
              Explanation

              Pakistan is the second-largest manufacturer of carpets in the world, after Iran.

              Pakistan's carpet industry is well-known for its high-quality handmade carpets, particularly those made in Lahore, Karachi, and Peshawar.

              Pakistani carpets are renowned for their intricate designs, vibrant colors, and exceptional craftsmanship.

              99

              Former CM of Punjab Usman Buzdar belongs to which city of Dera Ghazi Khan?

              Correct Answer: Taunsa Sharif
              Show explanation
              Explanation

              Usman Buzdar, the former Chief Minister of Punjab, hails from Taunsa Sharif, a city in the Dera Ghazi Khan district of Punjab, Pakistan.

              He served as the Chief Minister of Punjab from August 20, 2018, to April 30, 2022.

              100

              The Indus Valley Civilization belongs to the _____?

              Correct Answer: Neolithic age
              Show explanation
              Explanation

              The Indus Valley Civilization belongs to the Neolithic Age and Metal Age.

              The Indus Valley Civilisation (IVC), also known as the Indus Civilisation was a Bronze Age civilization in the northwestern regions of South Asia,

              lasting from 3300 BCE to 1300 BCE, and in its mature form from 2600 BCE to 1900 BCE.

              101

              Prime Minister Imran Khan inaugurated Langar Scheme Ehsas Programme in _____?

              Correct Answer: Islamabad
              Show explanation
              Explanation

              Prime Minister Imran Khan inaugurated the Langar Scheme Ehsas Programme in Islamabad on October 7, 2019.

              It was part of the Ehsaas Koi Bhooka Na Soye movement, which aimed to provide food to those in need.

              102

              Common Man's Gallery was inaugurated on 4th October 2019 by ______?

              Correct Answer: Asad Qaisar
              Show explanation
              Explanation

              The Common Man's Gallery was inaugurated by Asad Qaiser, the Speaker of the National Assembly, on October 4, 2019.

              103

              In the National Anthem of Pakistan " Jan-i-Istaqbal" stands for?

              Correct Answer: Inspiration of future
              Show explanation
              Explanation

              About National Anthem

              National Anthem was written by Hafeez Jalandhari in 1952 

              the music was produced by Ahmad G. Chagla in 1949.

              It was officially adopted as Pakistan's national anthem on 13 August 1954.

              Ahmed Rushdi recorded the National Anthem of Pakistan in 1954.

              After 68 years Pakistan's National Anthem recorded

              15 lines in the National Anthem of Pakistan

              48 Words in the National Anthem of Pakistan

              The National Anthem of Pakistan was played for the first time in front of Raza Shah Rahalvi of Iran

              In the National Anthem of Pakistan, " Jan-i-Istaqbal" stands for the Symbol of ambitions.

              the first national anthem of Pakistan was played on Pakistan's national radio on 14 August 1947

              80 sec is the duration of the National Anthem of Pakistan

              which language is Pakistan's national Anthem written in? Persian 


              104

              Who started the Hindu Mahasabha in 1921?

              Correct Answer: Madan Mohan Malaviya
              Show explanation
              Explanation

              The Hindu Mahasabha was founded in 1915.

              But it gained prominence in 1921.

              Madan Mohan Malaviya was one of its key founders and leaders. 

              Hindu Mahasabha is a political party in India. 

              Founded: 1915

              Founders: Vinayak Damodar Savarkar, Pandit Madan Mohan Malaviya, Lala Lajpat Rai

              105

              At the time of partition Qalat was a?

              Correct Answer: State
              Show explanation
              Explanation

              At the time of partition, Qalat was a State.

              Kalat was briefly independent from 12 August 1947 until 27 March 1948,

              When its ruler Ahmad Yar Khan acceded to Pakistan, making it one of the Princely states of Pakistan.


              Kalat or Qalat historically known as Qiqan

              They are predominantly Brahui-speaking

              They join Pakistan on 27 March 1948

              it is known locally as Kalat-e-Brahui and Kalat-e-Sewa

              It is known for Juniper old trees, the Moro Rive



              106

              Which dispute resolve between Pakistan and China in 1963?

              Correct Answer: Boundary issue
              Show explanation
              Explanation


              A boundary agreement was signed between Pakistan and China in 2 March 1963.

              Negotiations between the nations officially began on October 13, 1962,

              It was signed by:

              • Foreign Minister Chen Yi for the Chinese
              • Zulfikar Ali Bhutto for the Pakistani

              The eastern tip of the Wakhan Corridor starts the Sino-Pak border between the People's Republic of China and Pakistan.

              107

              Pakistan protested against construction of Baglihar Dam because _____?

              Correct Answer: It was violation of Indus Treaty Water
              Show explanation
              Explanation

              Pakistan protested against the construction of the Baghlihar Dam by India, claiming it violated the Indus Waters Treaty.

              This treaty regulates the use of river waters in the Indus Basin, and Pakistan argued that the dam would adversely affect its water supply.

              108

              Pakistan uses Palm oil in Banaspati Ghee Industry. From which country is Palm Oil mainly imported?

              Correct Answer: Malaysia
              Show explanation
              Explanation

              Pakistan mainly imports palm oil from Malaysia.

              It is one of the largest producers of palm oil globally.

              This oil is a key ingredient in the production of Banaspati ghee and other edible products in Pakistan.

              109

              The battle of Plassey was fought in 1757 by Robert Clive against?

              Correct Answer: Siraj din Dola
              Show explanation
              Explanation
              • War of Plassey, (23 June 1757). Victory for the British East India company inside the war of Plassey become the start of nearly two centuries of British rule in India.
              • For an occasion with such momentous effects, it turned into a fairly unimpressive military come across, the defeat of the Nawab of Bengal owing an awful lot to betrayal.
              • In India, Britain turned into represented by the British East India enterprise, a challenge that have been given a royal charter in 1600 to pursue trade inside the East Indies that protected the right to shape its very own army.
              • The French East India organization had a similar remit. From 1746, the rival agencies fought the Carnatic Wars for advantage in India, wherein they maintained trading posts, and sought impact over local rulers.
              110

              The Indian Muslim League opposed the Simon Commission. However a section of the Muslim League supported cooperation with Simon Commission. Who was the leader of this dissident section of the Muslim League?

              Correct Answer: Mian Muhammad Shafi
              Show explanation
              Explanation

              Mian Muhammad Shafi led the dissident section of the Muslim League that supported cooperation with the Simon Commission.

              • Simon's commission was rejected by Indian National Congress and  Muslim League
              • *
              • Simon Commission was appointed in 1928

              • It comprises 7 members of the British

              • It was opposed by both Jinnah & Gandhi
              111

              Name the princely which caused a Pakistan: state other than Kashmir dispute between India & Pakistan.

              Correct Answer: Junagadh
              Show explanation
              Explanation

              Junagadh was a princely state that decided to accede to Pakistan in 1947.

              This led to a major dispute between India and Pakistan.

              On September 14, 1947, Nawab Mahabat Khan of Junagadh signed the Instrument of Accession, declaring Junagadh as part of Pakistan.

              The dispute ultimately resulted in India's annexation of the state.

              112

              When was the 1973 constitution of Pakistan was promulgated?

              Correct Answer: August 14, 1973
              Show explanation
              Explanation

               The 1973 constitution of Pakistan was enacted on August 14, 1973


              Zulifqar               Ali Bhutto introduced a new constitution in Pakistan on August 14, 1973.


              According to the 1973 constitution, both the national assembly and senate elect the president of Pakistan

              whereas the prime minister is selected by the national assembly.


              The Bicameral Legislature system was introduced for the first time in Pakistan in the constitution of 1973.


              Khan Abdul Wali Khan was the opposition of the National Assembly at the time of promulgation.

              ND29-5-2023

              113

              Which other tragic event happened on 16th December in Pakistan?

              Correct Answer: Fall of Dhaka
              Show explanation
              Explanation

              The Fall of Dhaka occurred on 16th December 1971.

              On 16 December 1971, East Pakistan was liberated from Pakistan as the newly independent state of Bangladesh.

              Bangladesh is the new name of East Pakistan

              The history of East Bengal and East Pakistan from 1947 to 1971

              The territory of East Pakistan was handed in the Simla Agreement

              114

              Who is the founder of Sindh Institute of Urology and Transplantation (SIUT)?

              Correct Answer: Dr. Adeeb Rizvi
              Show explanation
              Explanation

              Dr. Adeeb Rizvi is the founder of the Sindh Institute of Urology and Transplantation (SIUT).

              It established in 1975 in Karachi, Pakistan.

              SIUT provides free medical care for urological diseases and promotes organ donation and transplantation.

              115

              The writer of the well-known book 'Mata-e-Zindagi' is:

              Correct Answer: Sadruddeen Ahmad Sadar
              Show explanation
              Explanation

              The writer of the well-known book "Mata-e-Zindagi" is Sadruddin Ahmad.

              Publication Date: The book was published in 1993.

              116

              To whom did the British sell Kashmir?

              Correct Answer: Gulab Singh
              Show explanation
              Explanation

              The British sold Kashmir to Gulab Singh. 


              After the First Anglo-Sikh War, the British East India Company defeated the Sikh Empire


              Through the Treaty of Amritsar in 1846, sold Kashmir to Gulab Singh for seven and a half million rupees.


              This transaction established Gulab Singh as the independent ruler of Jammu and Kashmir.

              117

              When was University of AlK established?

              Correct Answer: 1994
              Show explanation
              Explanation

              Al-Khair University Founded in 1994

              It is  located in  Bhimber, Azad Kashmir

              It is a private university

              They recognized by the Higher Education Commission (HEC), Govt. of Pakistan

              It was founded by Dr. Muhammad Bashir Goraya


              118

              In which year did Maharaja Gulab Singh finally acquire Kashmir?

              Correct Answer: 1846
              Show explanation
              Explanation

              Maharaja Gulab Singh finally acquired Kashmir in 1846.


              In 1846, Gulab Singh signed the Treaty of Amritsar with the British East India Company

              Which officially recognized him as the independent ruler of Jammu and Kashmir.

              The treaty followed the First Anglo-Sikh War (1845-1846)

              119

              Who is credited with spreading Islam in Kashmir?

              Correct Answer: Mir Syed Ali Hamdani
              Show explanation
              Explanation

              Mir Syed Ali Hamdani, also known as Shah-e-Hamadan,

              He is credited with spreading Islam in Kashmir through his teachings and influence in the 14th century.

              120

              How many times did Quaid-e-Azam visit Kashmir?

              Correct Answer: 4 times
              Show explanation
              Explanation

              Quaid-i-Azam Muhammad Ali Jinnah visited Kashmir at least four times.

              1929 - During this visit, Jinnah met with Kashmiri leaders and addressed their concerns.

              1932 - Jinnah visited to support the demands of the Kashmiris and to assess the political situation in the region.

              1936 - He made another visit to engage with local leaders and discuss political strategies.

              1944 - This visit was significant as Jinnah addressed the issues concerning the future of Jammu and Kashmir in the context of the ongoing independence movement.

              121

              When the first bus service between Srinagar and Muzaffarabad was started?

              Correct Answer: 2005
              Show explanation
              Explanation

              The first bus service between Srinagar and Muzaffarabad started in 2005.

              This service marked a significant step in improving connectivity between the regions in Kashmir and Pakistan-administered Azad Kashmir.

              122

              اشفاق احمد کس ملک کی یونیورسٹی میں اردو پڑھاتے رہے؟

              Correct Answer: اٹلی
              Show explanation
              Explanation

               اشفاق احمد نے ديال سنگھ کالج، لاہور میں دو سال تک اردو کے لیکچرر کے طور پر کام کیا

              اور بعد میں روم یونیورسٹی میں اردو کے استاد مقرر ہو گئے۔

              اعلیٖ تعلیم
              گورنمنٹ کالج لاہور سے ایم اے کیا،
              اٹلی کی روم یونیورسٹی اور گرے نوبلے یونیورسٹی فرانس سے اطالوی اور فرانسیسی زبان میں ڈپلومے کیے
              اور نیویارک یونیورسٹی سے براڈکاسٹنگ کی خصوصی تربیت حاصل کی۔
              123

              شوکت صدیقی کے کس ناول پر آدم جی ایوارڈ دیا گیا؟

              Correct Answer: خدا کی بستی
              Show explanation
              Explanation

              "خدا کی بستی" ناول شوکت صدیقی نے 1955 میں شائع کیا۔

              اس ناول کو غربت اور سماجی ناانصافی کے موضوعات پر مبنی ہونے کی وجہ سے بہت سراہا گیا۔

              124

              احمد ندیم قاسمی نے بی اے کا امتحان کہاں سے پاس کیا؟

              Correct Answer: صادق ایجرٹن کالج بہاولپور
              Show explanation
              Explanation

              احمد ندیم قاسمی

              گورنمنٹ مڈل اینڈ نارمل اسکول کیمبل پور (اٹک) (۱۹۲۱- ۱۹۲۵) تعلیم پائی

               گورنمنٹ ہائی اسکول شیخو پورہ سے(۱۹۳۰- ۱۹۳۱)میں میٹرک کیا

              صادق ایجرٹن کالج بہاولپور میں۱۹۳۱ داخل ہو گئے

              جہاں سے ۱۹۳۵ء میں بی اے کیا۔

              125

              پستان و گلستان کس کی کتابیں ہیں؟

              Correct Answer: شیخ سعدی
              Show explanation
              Explanation

              "بوستان" اور "گلستان" دونوں کتابیں شیخ سعدی کی مشہور تخلیقات ہیں:


              بوستان: یہ نظم کی شکل میں لکھی گئی ہے اور اس کا پہلا ایڈیشن 1257 میں شائع ہوا۔


              گلستان: یہ نثر کی شکل میں لکھی گئی ہے اور اس کا پہلا ایڈیشن 1258 میں شائع ہوا۔


              یہ دونوں کتابیں فارسی ادب میں اہم مقام رکھتی ہیں اور ان میں اخلاقی نصیحتیں اور حکایتیں شامل ہیں۔

              126

              کس مغل بادشاہ نے عمارات تعمیر کروائیں؟

              Correct Answer: شاہ جہاں
              Show explanation
              Explanation

               شاہ جہاں نے کئی مشہور عمارات تعمیر کروائیں، جن میں تاج محل، جامع مسجد، اور لال قلعہ شامل ہیں۔


              تاج محل:

              یہ عمارت اپنی خوبصورتی اور فن تعمیر کی وجہ سے دنیا بھر میں مشہور ہے اور اسے محبت کی علامت سمجھا جاتا ہے۔

              جامع مسجد:

              دہلی کی یہ مسجد اس دور کی سب سے بڑی اور اہم مساجد میں سے ایک ہے۔

              لال قلعہ:

              یہ قلعہ دہلی میں واقع ہے اور مغل فن تعمیر کا ایک شاندار نمونہ ہے۔

              یہ تمام عمارات مغل دور کی ثقافتی اور فنون لطیفہ کی عکاسی کرتی ہیں

              127

              آکادمی پنجاب کا قیام کس نے کیا؟

              Correct Answer: مولانا صلاح الدین احمد
              Show explanation
              Explanation

               آکادمی پنجاب کا قیام مولانا صلاح الدین احمد نے 1954 میں کیا تھا

              یہ ادارہ ادب اور زبان کی ترقی کے لیے اہم کام کرتا ہے، اور اس کا مقصد اردو زبان و ادب کی ترویج اور تحقیق کرنا ہے۔

              اس کے تحت مختلف ادبی سرگرمیاں، تقریبات، اور شائع کردہ مواد اردو ادب کی بہتر شناخت کے لیے اہم ہیں۔

              128

              سقراط کا آبائی پیشہ کون سا تھا؟

              Correct Answer: بت تراشی
              Show explanation
              Explanation

               سقراط کا آبائی پیشہ بت تراشی تھا۔

              وہ ایک یونانی فلسفی تھا جس نے اپنی ابتدائی زندگی میں بت تراشنے کا کام کیا۔


              129

              Sajji is a famous dish of which province of Pakistan?

              Correct Answer: Balochistan
              Show explanation
              Explanation

              Balochistan is known for the famous dish Sajji.

              It is traditionally made with whole lamb or chicken.

              It marinated and slow-cooked over an open fire.

              130

              Abdur Rehman Chugtai was a prominent name in the field of _____?

              Correct Answer: Painting
              Show explanation
              Explanation

              Abdur Rehman Chughtai was a renowned Pakistani painter.

              He is known for his distinctive Mughal-inspired artistic style.

              He is also notable for designing Pakistan's first postage stamp in 1947.

              131

              How many battles of Panipat have been there been?

              Correct Answer: Three
              Show explanation
              Explanation

              First Battle of Panipat: 21 April 1526

              Fought Between: Babur Vs Lodi

              Won by: Babur

              Second Battle of Panipat: November 5, 1556

              Fought Between: Hemu Vs Akbar

              Won by: Akbar

              Third Battle of Panipat: 14 January 1761

              Fought Between: Abdali Vs Marathas

              Won by: Abdali

              132

              Nmae th Leader from U.P who very first supported the Qarardad-e-Pakistan?

              Correct Answer: Ch Khaleeq-ul-Zaman
              Show explanation
              Explanation

              Chaudhry Khaleeq-ul-Zaman, a prominent leader from U.P

              He was one of the earliest supporters of the Lahore Resolution, also known as the Qarardad-e-Pakistan, passed in 1940

              He played a vital role in the politics of the All-India Muslim League.

              Born on 25 December 1889 ( Uttar Pradesh, India)

              Died on18 May 1973 (aged 83)

              133

              Who was the first Non-Muslim to have won the coveted Sword of Honor at the Pakistan Military Academy, Kakul?

              Correct Answer: Captain Esmond D. Cunha
              Show explanation
              Explanation

              Captain Esmond D. Cunha was the first non-Muslim to win the coveted Sword of Honour at the Pakistan Military Academy, Kakul.

              He achieved this prestigious award in 1951, marking a significant milestone in Pakistan's military history.

              134

              The educational meeting in 1947 is called?

              Correct Answer: Conference
              Show explanation
              Explanation

              This conference addressed educational reforms post-independence


              The first National Education Conference was held in Karachi 


              It was held from 27th November 1947 to 1st December 1947.

              It was headed by Quaid-e-Azam Muhammad Ali Jinnah.

              135

              After which incident, Sir Syed Ahmad Khan predicted that gulf of differences between Hindus and Muslims of India shall widen with the passage of time?

              Correct Answer: Urdu Hindu controversy in UP
              Show explanation
              Explanation

              Sir Syed Ahmad Khan predicted the widening gulf between Hindus and Muslims after the Urdu-Hindu controversy in UP in 1867.

              The controversy arose when Hindus demanded that Hindi replace Urdu as the official language in courts, which deepened religious and cultural divides.

              136

              Lahore is famous as ____?

              Correct Answer: City of Colleges
              Show explanation
              Explanation

              Lahore is called the "City of Colleges" because this city has the greatest number of colleges and universities.

              University of the Punjab ranks #1 in Lahore

              The University of Lahore is recognized as the largest private-sector university in Pakistan

              The Oldest University of Lahore is: King Edward Medical University (1860)

              __
              Lahore is the second largest city in Pakistan

              Lahore is the 26th largest in the world

              Lahore is the capital of the province of Punjab,


              ND-6-09-2023

              137

              Which Government body is responsible for regulating electronic media?

              Correct Answer: PERMA
              Show explanation
              Explanation

              The "Pakistan Electronic Media Regulatory Authority (PEMRA)" was established in 2002.

              It was formed to regulate and oversee the electronic media in Pakistan.

              It ensuring compliance with broadcasting standards and regulations.

              138

              On 22nd November 2016, former Caretaker Prime Minister of Pakistan passed away. What was his name?

              Correct Answer: Moin Qureshi
              Show explanation
              Explanation

              The former Caretaker Prime Minister of Pakistan who passed away on November 22, 2016, was Moeenuddin Ahmad Qureshi.

              He was the third Caretaker Prime Minister of Pakistan.

              He remain 18 July 1993 to 19 Oct 1993  Caretaker PM.

              Qureshi also served as the senior vice president of World Bank.

              139

              Name the only Pakistani who has served as the Secretary General of the OIC?

              Correct Answer: Syed Sharifuddin Pirzada
              Show explanation
              Explanation

              The Secretaries-General of the OIC since its establishment are:

              1. H.R.H. Tunku Abdul Rahman (Malaysia): 1971-1973
              2. H.E. Hassan Al-Touhami (Egypt): 1974-1975
              3. H.E. Dr. Amadou Karim Gaye (Senegal): 1975-1979
              4. H.E. Mr. Habib Chatty (Tunisia): 1979-1984
              5. H.E. Syed Sharifuddin Pirzada (Pakistan): 1985-1988
              140

              Martial law was imposed in Lahore in which year?

              Correct Answer: 1953
              Show explanation
              Explanation

              Martial law was imposed in Lahore in 1953.

              The martial law was imposed to control the sectarian riots against the Ahmadiyya Muslim Community.

              The Pakistan Army was called for the first time to control a civil strife.

              The martial law was imposed on March 6, 1953.

              The martial law was lifted on May 14, 1953.

              The Prime Minister of Pakistan during this time was Khwaja Nazimuddin.

              The Governor-General of Pakistan during this time was Malik Ghulam Muhammad.

              141

              Azad Kashmir Government was set up on ______?

              Correct Answer: October 24, 1947
              Show explanation
              Explanation
              • Azad Kashmir Day is celebrated on October 24, which is the day that the Azad Jammu and Kashmir government was created in 1947.
              • On October 24, a second provisional government of Azad Kashmir was established at Palandri under the leadership of Sardar Ibrahim Khan.
              142

              In 1974 the famous Heavyweight boxer, Muhammad Ali, regained the Heavyweight title by defeating _____?

              Correct Answer: Joe Frazier
              Show explanation
              Explanation

              Muhammad Ali regained the Heavyweight title in 1974 by defeating George Foreman in the famous "Rumble in the Jungle" fight.

              Date: October 30, 1974

              Location: Kinshasa, Zaire (now the Democratic Republic of Congo)

              Opponent: George Foreman

              Result: Muhammad Ali won by knockout in the 8th round, regaining the Heavyweight title.

              143

              At the time of independence, there were numerous princely states in India. Which state was the biggest in area?

              Correct Answer: Jammu and Kashmir
              Show explanation
              Explanation
              The largest Princely state by area at the time of partition was Kashmir.

              Out of 562 princely states during 1947, 13 princely states decided to join Pakistan.

              ****

              These 13 princely states are:

                  Bahawalpur: On 3 October 1947, the Nawab of Bahawalpur, Sadeq Mohammad Khan V, acceded his state to Pakistan, becoming the first ruler to do so successfully.

                  Khairpur: The state of Khairpur acceded to Pakistan on 3 October 1947.

                  Chitral: The Mehtar of Chitral, Muzaffar-ul-Mulk, stated his intention to accede to Pakistan on 15 August 1947. On 28 July 1969, President Yahya Khan announced the full integration.

                  144

                  Name the soldier of Pakistan Army who spent forty years in Indian jails and now has passed away on 28th August 2018 in Pakistan?

                  Correct Answer: Maqbool Hussain
                  Show explanation
                  Explanation

                  Maqbool Hussain, a Pakistani soldier, was captured by Indian forces in 1975 and spent 40 years in Indian jails.

                  He was released in 2015 and returned to Pakistan, where he passed away on August 28, 2018, at the age of 68.

                  145

                  Which of the following cities received Hilal-e-Istiqlal for it's heroic resistance during 1965 Indo-Pak war?

                  Correct Answer: Sialkot
                  Show explanation
                  Explanation

                  In 1966, the Government of Pakistan awarded Hilal-i-Istaqlal to Lahore, Sargodha, and Sialkot.

                  These cities showed severe resistance to the enemy during the Indo-Pakistani War of 1965.

                  They were key targets of the Indian advance.

                  146

                  Who was the last Nizam of Hyderabad?

                  Correct Answer: Mir Osman Ali Khan
                  Show explanation
                  Explanation

                  Mir Osman Ali Khan was the last Nizam of Hyderabad.

                  He ruling from 1911 until the annexation of Hyderabad by India in 1948.

                  1st Nizam: Mir Qamar-ud-Din Siddiqi (Nizam-ul-Mulk) – 1724 to 1748.

                  2nd Nizam: Mir Nizam Ali Pasha – 1748 to 1803.

                  3rd Nizam: Mir Akbar Ali Pasha – 1803 to 1829.

                  147

                  Who wrote The Indus Saga and Making of Pakistan”?

                  Correct Answer: Aitzaz Ahsan
                  Show explanation
                  Explanation

                  Chaudhry Aitzaz Ahsan is a Pakistani politician and lawyer.

                  He served two times as the Leader of the House in the Senate of Pakistan from 1994 to 1997 and again from 2012 to 2015.

                  148

                  In which year did Mohtarma Fatima Jinnah contest the Presidential election?

                  Correct Answer: 1965
                  Show explanation
                  Explanation
                  • Fatima Jinnah ran for the presidency of Pakistan in the 1965 elections.
                  • Challenging the incumbent President Ayub Khan.
                  149

                  The Soan Valley is the site of an ancient civilization and is situated in _____?

                  Correct Answer: Pothohar Plateau
                  Show explanation
                  Explanation

                  The Soan Valley, known for its ancient civilization, is located in the Pothohar Plateau region of Pakistan, near the northern part of the Punjab province.

                  The Soan Valley is also home to the Soan River.

                  It flows through the valley and supports a diverse range of flora and fauna.

                  150

                  In which area Malala Yousafzai and her two friends were shot by the terrorist after returning from school?

                  Correct Answer: Swat
                  Show explanation
                  Explanation

                  Malala Yousafzai and her two friends, Kainat Riaz and Shazia Ramzan, were shot by the Taliban on October 9, 2012, in Swat Valley, Pakistan, after returning from school.

                  Malala was targeted for her advocacy on girls' education and her vocal opposition to the Taliban's efforts to ban girls from attending school.

                  151

                  The 10th International Defence Exhibition and Seminar (IDEAS) 2018 was held between _____?

                  Correct Answer: 27 - 30 November 2018
                  Show explanation
                  Explanation

                  The 10th International Defence Exhibition and Seminar (IDEAS) 2018

                  It was held from 27 to 30 November 2018 at the Karachi Expo Centre in Pakistan.

                  Participation from over 50 countries, including China, Russia, Turkey, and the United States, among others.

                  152

                  Abdullah Yousaf Ali was known as _____?

                  Correct Answer: Principal of Islamia College Lahore
                  Show explanation
                  Explanation

                  Abdullah Yusuf Ali (1872-1953) was a British-Indian Islamic scholar, translator, and educator.
