                        Pakistan's Ahsan Ayaz has won Colleyville Open Squash 2024

                        He defeated Indian opponent Yash Bhargava in squash tournament final

                        Colleyville Open Squash held in August 2024

                        The Colleyville Open Squash Tournament had a total prize money of $3,000.

                         ****

                        269

                        Fall of Constantinople leads to which history?

                        Correct Answer: Modern history
                        Show explanation
                        Explanation

                        The Fall of Constantinople in 1453 is considered to mark the end of the Middle Ages and the beginning of Modern history.


                        The city's conquest by the Ottoman Empire had profound effects on European trade 

                        270

                        Which US President visited Pakistan in 1969?

                        Correct Answer: Richard M. Nixon
                        Show explanation
                        Explanation

                        Richard Nixon
                        37th President of the United States
                        from January 20, 1969 – August 9, 1974

                        July 31 – August 1, 1969
                         India
                        New Delhi
                        State visit. Met with Acting President Mohammad Hidayatullah. Prime Minister is Indira Gandhi
                        August 1–2, 1969
                         Pakistan
                        Lahore
                        State visit. Met with President Yahya Khan.

                        list of  US Presidents who visit Pakistan:
                        Name
                        Locale
                        Remarks
                        Date
                        Dwight D. Eisenhower
                        Karachi
                        Informal visit; met with President Ayub Khan.
                        December 7–9, 1959
                        Lyndon B. Johnson
                        Karachi
                        Met with President Ayub Khan.
                        December 23, 1967
                        William J. Clinton
                        Islamabad
                        Met with President Tarar and General Musharraf; delivered radio address.
                        March 25, 2000
                        George W. Bush
                        Islamabad
                        Met with President Musharraf.
                        March 3–4, 2006
                        271

                        Pakistan receives rainfall in the months of July to September.

                        Correct Answer: Monsoon
                        Show explanation
                        Explanation

                        Monsoon season in Pakistan starts in July and ends in September.

                        It bringing significant rainfall mainly to northern and eastern regions. 

                        Additional information:

                        Cool, dry winter: December to February

                        Hot, dry spring: March to May

                        Summer rainy season (southwest monsoon): June to September

                        Retreating monsoons: October to November

                        272

                        Which personality associated with Kartarpur:

                        Correct Answer: Baba Guru Nanak
                        Show explanation
                        Explanation

                        The first guru of Sikhism, Guru Nanak, founded Kartarpur in 1504 CE

                        On the right bank of the Ravi River and established the first Sikh commune there.


                        Additional information 

                        The Gurdwara Darbar Sahib Kartarpur is located in the Narowal.

                        Located on the right bank of the Ravi River.

                        It lies near the India-Pakistan border.

                        The Kartarpur Corridor was introduced by PM Modi and his Pakistani counterpart Imran Khan.

                        On the 550th birthday anniversary of Guru Nanak in November 2019.

                        It is 4.7 kilometers long highway that connects:

                        • The Dera Baba Nanak shrine in India.
                        • The Darbar Sahib Kartarpur in Pakistan.
                        273

                        The Delhi Agreement was agreement signed between a trilateral ______?

                        Correct Answer: India, Pakistan and Bangladesh
                        Show explanation
                        Explanation

                        The Delhi Agreement pak1973 was a trilateral agreement between India, Pakistan, and Bangladesh.

                        It was signed on August 28, 1973

                        To resolve post-war issues such as prisoner exchanges.

                        It officials held in the three countries after the 1971 Bangladesh Liberation War.

                        Transfer of Population Under the Terms of the Delhi Agreement

                        ContextCold War
                        Drafted17 April 1973
                        Signed9 April 1973; 51 years ago
                        LocationNew Delhi, India
                        Sealed19 September 1973
                        Effective28 August 1973
                        ConditionRatification by both parties
                        Expiration28 August 1974
                        Expiry1 July 1974
                        DepositaryIndira Gandhi (Prime Minister of India)
                        Zulfikar Ali Bhutto (Prime Minister of Pakistan)


                          274

                          Following leaders led the Muslim League's delegation for Simla Conference?

                          Correct Answer: Jinnah, Liaquat and Nizamuddin
                          Show explanation
                          Explanation

                          The Muslim League's delegation for the Simla Conference in 1945 was led by:

                          1. Muhammad Ali Jinnah (the Quaid-e-Azam)
                          2. Liaquat Ali Khan (a close associate of Jinnah and later the first Prime Minister of Pakistan)
                          3. Khwaja Nazimuddin (a prominent politician and later the second Prime Minister of Pakistan)

                          These leaders played a crucial role in representing the Muslim League's interests and demands during the conference.

                          275

                          To whom General Pervaiz Musharraf replaced?

                          Correct Answer: General Jahangir Karamat
                          Show explanation
                          Explanation

                          General Pervez Musharraf replaced General Jehangir Karamat as the Chief of Army Staff of Pakistan in 1998.

                          General Jehangir Karamat was a prominent military leader who served as the Chief of Army Staff from 1996 to 1998.

                          He was replaced by General Pervez Musharraf, who later became the President of Pakistan.

                          276

                          Agreement of CPEC was signed during Chinese President's visit to Pakistan in ______?

                          Correct Answer: April 2015
                          Show explanation
                          Explanation

                          The agreement for the China-Pakistan Economic Corridor (CPEC) was signed during Chinese President Xi Jinping's visit to Pakistan in April 2015.

                          The China-Pakistan Economic Corridor (CPEC) is a series of energy and infrastructure projects, some of which connect China’s Xinjiang to Gwadar.

                          Initiated in mid-2013 and formally launched in April 2015.

                          CPEC is generally described as a $46 billion package of Chinese aid.

                          277

                          The vale of Peshawar is drained by _____ river.

                          Correct Answer: Kabul
                          Show explanation
                          Explanation

                          The Vale of Peshawar is drained by the Kabul River.

                          It is a major river that flows from Afghanistan into Pakistan.

                          It passing through Peshawar and eventually joining the Indus River.

                          It is 7,176 km2 (2,771 sq mi) in area.

                          It has a mean elevation of 345 metres (1,132 ft). 

                          278

                          "Pakistan: The Heart of Asia" was written by _____?

                          Correct Answer: Liaquat Ali Khan
                          Show explanation
                          Explanation

                          Pakistan: The Heart of Asia” was written by Liaquat Ali Khan.

                          Additional Information

                          "Pakistan: The Heart of Asia" was indeed written by Liaquat Ali Khan,

                          who served as the first Prime Minister of Pakistan after the country gained independence in 1947.

                          The book discusses various aspects of Pakistan's history, culture, and significance in the context of the broader Asian region.


                          279

                          Pakistan Female Army Officers awarded UN Medal for exemplary services in which country on 1st February 2020?

                          Correct Answer: Congo
                          Show explanation
                          Explanation

                          On 1st February 2020, Pakistan's first-ever all-female peacekeeping team awarded UN Medal in Congo.

                          This team of 15 female officers, who serve at the ranks of Major and Captain.

                          280

                          The main responsibilities of Security and Exchange Commission of Pakistan are ______?

                          Correct Answer: All of these
                          Show explanation
                          Explanation

                          The Securities and Exchange Commission of Pakistan (SECP) regulates Pakistan's securities market and corporate sector. Its main responsibilities are:

                          • Supervising the capital market, including stock exchanges and brokerages.
                          • Regulating non-banking finance companies (NBFCs) such as leasing firms and mutual funds.
                          • Enforcing corporate governance and securities regulations for listed companies and public entities.
                          281

                          The dominant source of employment in Pakistan is ____?

                          Correct Answer: Agriculture
                          Show explanation
                          Explanation

                           Agriculture constitutes the largest sector of our economy.

                          The majority of the population, directly or indirectly, is dependent on this sector.

                          ND23-4-2023


                          282

                          Which of the provinces is the smallest in population?

                          Correct Answer: Baluchistan
                          Show explanation
                          Explanation

                          Balochistan is the smallest province by Population 

                          It is the biggest province of Pakistan by area.

                          They cover 42 percent of the area of Pakistan

                          It is located in the west of Pakistan.

                            It became a province in 1970.

                              Capital of Baluchistan is Quetta.

                              35 districts are there in Balochistan

                              Baluchistan province has 7 divisions.

                                            • Kalat
                                            • Makran
                                            • Nasirabad
                                            • Quetta
                                            • Sibi
                                            • Zhob
                                            • Rakshan
                                            283

                                            Which is the major source of water in Pakistan?

                                            Correct Answer: Rivers
                                            Show explanation
                                            Explanation

                                            The Indus River is the major source of water for the more than 180 million people of Pakistan.

                                            The Indus River starts in Tibet

                                            The river Indus originates from the Mansarovar Lake

                                            The Indus River flows through India, Pakistan,

                                            The Indus River finally falls into the Arabian Sea near Karachi.

                                            River Indus starts making a delta near the city of Thatta

                                            The old name of the Indus river was: Sindhu

                                            National River of Pakistan: Indus River

                                            6 Barrages constructed in the Indus River

                                            Indus River Length: 3,180 km

                                            Indus River 2nd source: Gar Tsangpo

                                            The upper Indus Plain ends at Mithankot 

                                            The upper Indus Plain starts from Kalabagh

                                            ND23-4-2023

                                            284

                                            Greenwich main time is ___ hours behind of Pakistan standard time.

                                            Correct Answer: 5
                                            Show explanation
                                            Explanation

                                            Greenwich main time is 5 hours behind Pakistan standard time.


                                            Greenwich Mean Time (GMT) is the mean solar time at the Royal Observatory in Greenwich, London, counted from midnight.

                                            Greenwich Mean Time was established in 1884

                                            285

                                            According to Article 47 of the Constitution the President Pakistan can be _____?

                                            Correct Answer: Impeached
                                            Show explanation
                                            Explanation

                                            According to Article 47 of the Constitution of Pakistan, the President can be impeached on charges of violating the Constitution or gross misconduct, upon a two-thirds majority vote by Parliament.

                                            286

                                            ______ is a narrow strip of territory which separates Pakistan, Afghanistan, China and Tajikistan.

                                            Correct Answer: Wakhan
                                            Show explanation
                                            Explanation

                                            The Wakhan Corridor is a field in the northeastern part of Afghanistan that extends into China and separates Tajikistan and Pakistan.    


                                            287

                                            In Pakistan the Governor is answerable to the _____?

                                            Correct Answer: President
                                            Show explanation
                                            Explanation

                                            In Pakistan, the Governor is answerable to the President.

                                            As per Article 105 of the Constitution, which outlines the Governor's role and responsibilities in the provincial government.

                                            288

                                            When Pakistan shifted capital from Karachi to Islamabad?

                                            Correct Answer: 1967
                                            Show explanation
                                            Explanation

                                            Islamabad officially became the capital of Pakistan on 14 August 1967.

                                            Ayoub Khan made Islamabad the capital of Pakistan

                                            The area of Islamabad before its declaration as the capital of Pakistan was known as Raj Shahi.

                                            Islamabad is the country's ninth-most populous city, with a population of over 1.2 million people

                                            Constructed 1960

                                            Postcode 44000

                                            ND23-4-2023

                                            AGM 09-2-2023


                                            289

                                            Name the area of Kashmir where a spontaneous armed uprising took place against the Dogra tyranny, on the eve of partition?

                                            Correct Answer: Poonch
                                            Show explanation
                                            Explanation

                                            In 1947, a spontaneous armed uprising against the Dogra tyranny occurred in Poonch, Kashmir.

                                            As the region sought to challenge the rule of the Dogra monarchy on the eve of partition.


                                            The Dogra dynasty was a dynasty of Hindu Rajputs who ruled Jammu & Kashmir from 1846 to 1947.

                                            After the First Anglo-Sikh War in 1846, the British gave Kashmir and the title of 'Maharaja' to Gulab Singh

                                            291

                                            On what date did the major earthquake hit the Northern Areas and Azad Kashmir in 2005?

                                            Correct Answer: 8th October
                                            Show explanation
                                            Explanation

                                            The earthquake of October 2005 was result of Tectonic dislocation.

                                            The 2005 Kashmir earthquake occurred at 08:50:39 Pakistan Standard Time on 8 October in Pakistani-administered Azad Kashmir.

                                            Magnitude: 7.6 Mw

                                            More than 80,000 people perished as a result of the quake.

                                            While an estimated 4 million others were left homeless.

                                            292

                                            Which was entrusted with the job of dividing the Army in 1947?

                                            Correct Answer: Field Marshal Auchinleck
                                            Show explanation
                                            Explanation

                                            Field Marshal Auchinleck was entrusted with the job of dividing the British Indian Army in 1947 during the partition of India and Pakistan.

                                            He was responsible for overseeing the distribution of military assets between the two new nations.

                                            293

                                            Who was the mediator of Tashkent declaration?

                                            Correct Answer: Soviet Union (USSR)
                                            Show explanation
                                            Explanation

                                            The Tashkent Declaration was signed between India and Pakistan on 10 January 1966 to resolve the Indo-Pakistani War of 1965

                                            The Tashkent Declaration was a peace agreement between India and Pakistan. 

                                            Through the mediation of the Soviet Union Tashkent Agreement held between India and Pakistan 

                                            Tashkent Declaration was signed president of Pakistan Ayub Khan and by India’s Prime Minister Lal Bahadur Shastri 

                                            ND11-2-2023

                                            294

                                            After Independence the first Radio Station was established in?

                                            Correct Answer: Karachi
                                            Show explanation
                                            Explanation


                                            Pakistan’s first radio station was set up in Karachi.

                                            Karachi radio station was inaugurated by Liaqat Ali Khan on 14 August 1948


                                            Additional Information:

                                            Pakistan's independence was announced first through Lahore Radio station.

                                            Three radio stations existed at the time of independence of Pakistan on 14 August 1947.

                                            1. Peshawar Radio Station was established in 1935

                                            2. Lahore radio station established in 1937

                                            3. Dhaka radio station established in 1939


                                            ******

                                            295

                                            Which of the following was constructed by Emperor Shah Jahan?

                                            Correct Answer: Shalimar Garden
                                            Show explanation
                                            Explanation

                                            Facts about Shalimar Garden

                                            Mughal Emperor Shah Jahan built Shalimar Gardens Lahore.

                                            Shalimar Gardens Lahore was built in the year 1641-42.

                                            Shalimar Gardens Lahore Included in World Heritage Site: 1981

                                            Monuments like the Taj Mahal, the Jama Masjid, the Red Fort, etc. were also built by Shah Jahan.


                                            Sr#

                                            Built by

                                            Name of Places

                                            1

                                            Jahangir​

                                            Shalimar Bagh, Kashmir

                                            2

                                            Shah Jahan

                                            1. Shalimar Garden, Pakistan,

                                             

                                            2. Taj Mahal,

                                            3. the Jama Masjid,

                                            4. the Red Fort

                                            3

                                            Akbar

                                            1.    Buland Darwaza,

                                            2.    Fatehpur Sikri

                                            4

                                            Aurangzeb

                                            Moti Masjid, Delhi

                                            296

                                            Which of the following died Natural death?

                                            Correct Answer: Nehru
                                            Show explanation
                                            Explanation

                                            Among the listed leaders, Jawaharlal Nehru died a natural death in 1964.
                                            Mahatma Gandhi was assassinated in 1948.
                                            Indira Gandhi was assassinated in 1984.
                                            Rajiv Gandhi was assassinated in 1991.
                                            Nehru was the first Prime Minister of India and served from 1947 to 1964.

                                            297

                                            What is the significance of 17 August 1988 in Pakistan's history?

                                            Correct Answer: Gen: Zia ul Haq died in a plane crash
                                            Show explanation
                                            Explanation

                                            Zia-Ul-Haq Died on 17-08-1988 by Plane crashed in Bahawalpur

                                            American Ambassador Arnold Raphael with President Zia Ul Haque in a plane crash

                                            General Muhammad Zia-ul-Haq HI, GCSJ, ร.ม.ภ, was a Pakistani four-star general 

                                            Muhammad Zia-Ul-Haq became the CMLA of Pakistan in: 1977

                                            Born: August 12, 1924, Jalandhar, India

                                            Muhammad Zia-ul-Haq took oath as President of Pakistan on 23 March 1985.

                                            Muhammad Zia-ul-Haq becomes 6th President of Pakistan.

                                            General Zia Ul Haq ruled Pakistan from 1977 to 1988

                                            The provincial constitution order martial law in 1977.

                                            When PCO was introduced by Zia ul Haq? 1981

                                            When General Zia Ul Haq addressed in UN? 23 Oct 1985

                                            He remains the country's longest-serving de facto head of state and Chief of Army Staff

                                            Ghulam Ishaq Khan succeeded Zia Ul Haque as President of Pakistan.

                                            In the Era of Zia ul Haq who was the Prime Minister of Pakistan: Muhammad Khan Junejo

                                            ND13-1-2023


                                            298

                                            The caste system in the subcontinent, was first established by the _____?

                                            Correct Answer: Aryans
                                            Show explanation
                                            Explanation

                                            The caste system in the Indian subcontinent was first established by the Aryans, who arrived in the region around 1500 BCE.

                                            They created a social hierarchy with four varnas (classes):

                                            1. Brahmins (priests and scholars)
                                            2. Kshatriyas (warriors and rulers)
                                            3. Vaishyas (merchants and traders)
                                            4. Shudras (labourers and servants)
                                            299

                                            Diamir Bhasha Dam is located in which city of Pakistan?

                                            Correct Answer: Gilgit
                                            Show explanation
                                            Explanation

                                            Diamer-Bhasha Dam is constructed, on the River Indus 

                                            between Kohistan district in Khyber Pakhtunkhwa and Diamer district in Gilgit Baltistan

                                             Its foundation stone was laid by the then Prime Minister of Pakistan in 1998

                                            Opened: 2028

                                            Height: 272 m

                                            Names DamLocation Rivercompleted
                                            Rawal DamIslamabad Korang River1962
                                            Mangla DamMirpur District - AJKJhelum River1967
                                            Tarbela DamHaripur - KPKIndus River1974
                                            Hub DamLasbela Districts SindhHub River1979
                                            Khanpur DamHaripur - KPKHaro River1985
                                            Diamer Bhasha DamGilgit Baltistan - KPKIndus RiverUnder Con.
                                            Dasu Dam Kohistan District - KPKIndus River

                                            300

                                            What was the distance of Arshad Nadeem's Olympic record-breaking throw at the Paris 2024 Olympics?

                                            Correct Answer: 92.97 meters
                                            Show explanation
                                            Explanation

                                            Arshad Nadeem set a new Olympic record by clinching the coveted gold medal in the men’s javelin final

                                            This ended Pakistan’s 32-year wait for success at the Games 

                                            Throw: 92.97-m throw

                                            Winning date: 8 Aug 2024

                                            Arshad belongs to Mian Channu.

                                             Nadeem’s throw was Pakistan’s first individual gold medal

                                            Note: It also shattered the previous Olympic record of 90.57m, set by the Netherlands’ Andreas Thorkildsen at the Beijing 2008 Olympics.

                                            The throw now stands as the sixth longest throw ever, and the best in the world this year.

                                             ****

                                            Iqbal's first book of poetry in Urdu, Bang-i-Dara (1924), was followed by Bal-i-Jibril in 1935 and Zarb-i-Kalim in 1936.

                                            What is Nishat Group known for?

                                            Correct Answer: Textile and Energy

                                            The company was founded by Pakistani business magnate Mian Muhammad Yahya in 1951


                                            Its headquartered in Lahore

                                            308

                                            Tilla Jogian is located in which geographical region?

                                            Correct Answer: Pothohar Plateau
                                            Show explanation
                                            Explanation

                                            Tilla Jogian is a historic and religious site situated in the Pothohar Plateau, Punjab,.

                                            Mountain in the Salt Range of Pakistan's Punjab province.

                                            The complex was the most important center for Hindu jogis in Punjab before 1947. 

                                            Height: 975 meters 

                                            Abandoned: 1947

                                            Part of: Mountain


                                            309

                                            In 1907, Mohsin-ul-Mulk served as the secretary of which organization?

                                            Correct Answer: All India Muslim League
                                            Show explanation
                                            Explanation

                                            Nawab Mohsin-ul-Mulk, was an Indian Muslim politician.

                                            Other names: Syed Mehdi Ali, Munir Nawaz Jang

                                            He was involved in the Aligarh Movement and was one of the founders of the All-India Muslim League in 1906.

                                            Born: December 9, 1837, Etawah, India

                                            Died: October 16, 1907, Shimla, India

                                            Party: All-India Muslim League


                                            310

                                            The first Urdu newspaper was issued from which city?

                                            Correct Answer: Calcutta
                                            Show explanation
                                            Explanation

                                            The first Urdu newspaper called "Jam-e-Jahan-Numa"

                                            It was published from Calcutta (now Kolkata) in 1822.

                                            It marked the beginning of Urdu journalism in the subcontinent.

                                            The Editor of Jam-i-Jahan-Numa by Harihar Dutta.


                                            311

                                            چند ہم عصر خانوں پر مبنی معروف کتاب ہے۔ اس کے مصنف کون ہے

                                            Correct Answer: مولوی عبدالحق
                                            Show explanation
                                            Explanation

                                            مولوی عبدالحق کی کتاب "چاند ہم عصر" اردو ادب کی ایک مشہور تصنیف ہے۔

                                            اس کتاب کی اشاعت 1952 میں ہوئی تھی۔


                                            312

                                            The border line area between Pakistan and India at Sir Creek, a disputed area of Sindh, is called:

                                            Correct Answer: Green Line
                                            Show explanation
                                            Explanation

                                            Indo-Pakistani Border Dispute. The Green Line is the boundary as claimed by Pakistan, the Red Line is the boundary as claimed by India.


                                            Additional information:

                                            • Sir Creek is located between Pakistan & India
                                            • It is located along the southeastern border of the Sindh province of Pakistan and the western border of the Gujarat province in India.
                                            • This area is called the Greater Rann of Kutch, a marshland situated along the Arabian Sea.
                                            • ***
                                            • Sir Creek is important to Pakistan because: It is environmentally important to prevent coastal wetlands from degradation
                                            • About 90 km2 of the lake comes under Pakistan, whilst the majority of the lake i.e. 210 km2, lies within India.
                                            313

                                            Labour Judiciary is ______?

                                            Correct Answer: All of them
                                            Show explanation
                                            Explanation

                                            The labor judiciary in Pakistan includes:

                                            • Local Labour Courts
                                            • Labour Appellate Tribunals
                                            • The National Industrial Relations Commission

                                            These handle labor-related disputes and issues.

                                            314

                                            A strike/lockout is illegal if it is _____?

                                            Correct Answer: All of them
                                            Show explanation
                                            Explanation

                                            A strike or lockout is illegal in Pakistan if it is:

                                            • Commenced without notice
                                            • Continued despite prohibition orders
                                            • During the period when a settlement or award is in effect
                                            315

                                            According to Pakistan laws "Collective Bargaining" is _____?

                                            Correct Answer: Legal
                                            Show explanation
                                            Explanation

                                            In Pakistan, "Collective Bargaining" is legal under the Industrial Relations Act.

                                            It allowing workers to negotiate terms with employers through their representative unions.

                                            316

                                            Application for registration of a trade union is to be submitted before _____?

                                            Correct Answer: Registrar of Trade Unions
                                            Show explanation
                                            Explanation

                                            Applications for the registration of a trade union in Pakistan are to be submitted to the Registrar of Trade Unions.

                                            He is responsible for overseeing the registration process.

                                            317

                                            Article 17 of the Pakistan's Constitution ____?

                                            Correct Answer: Allows every citizen to form associations or unions
                                            Show explanation
                                            Explanation

                                            Article 17 of Pakistan's Constitution specifically allows every citizen to form associations or unions.

                                            It ensuring the right to assemble and organize.

                                            318

                                            Which Article of the Constitution of the Islamic Republic of Pakistan prohibits all forms of forced labour?

                                            Correct Answer: 11
                                            Show explanation
                                            Explanation

                                            Article 11 of the Constitution of the Islamic Republic of Pakistan prohibits all forms of forced labor.

                                            It ensuring that no individual can be subjected to involuntary work or exploitation.

                                            319

                                            Pakistani Labour Laws define a 'Child' as anyone under the age of _____?

                                            Correct Answer: 15
                                            Show explanation
                                            Explanation

                                            According to Pakistani labor laws, a 'Child' is defined as anyone under the age of 15.

                                            Child labor is prohibited for those below this age in hazardous occupations.

                                            320

                                            In result of Supreme Court's decision in 1988, the Bonded Labour Abolition Act was passed in ______?

                                            Correct Answer: 1992
                                            Show explanation
                                            Explanation

                                            The Bonded Labour System (Abolition) Act was passed in 1992 as a result of the Supreme Court's decision in 1988.

                                            It aimed at eradicating bonded labor practices in Pakistan.

                                            321

                                            Under Article 160 of the Constitution the distribution of Revenues between the Federation and the Province is made by ______?

                                            Correct Answer: National Finance Commission
                                            Show explanation
                                            Explanation

                                            Under Article 160 of the Constitution of Pakistan, the distribution of revenues between the Federation and the Provinces is determined by the National Finance Commission (NFC).

                                            It makes recommendations for the financial allocation.

                                            322

                                            A provincial govt. can construct power houses and grid stations and lay transmission lines for use within the province?

                                            Correct Answer: Yes
                                            Show explanation
                                            Explanation

                                            Under the Constitution of Pakistan, provincial governments are allowed to construct power houses, grid stations, and lay transmission lines for use within the province as part of their jurisdiction over energy resources.

                                            323

                                            Only that person can be appointed as Attorney General of Pakistan who is eligible for appointment is ______?

                                            Correct Answer: Judge of the Supreme Court
                                            Show explanation
                                            Explanation

                                            The Attorney General of Pakistan must be a person who is eligible to be appointed as a Judge of the Supreme Court.

                                            It requiring the same qualifications as a Supreme Court judge.

                                            324

                                            A person shall be disqualified from being elected or chosen as, and from being a member of the parliament if _____?

                                            Correct Answer: All of these
                                            Show explanation
                                            Explanation

                                            A person shall be disqualified from being elected or chosen as, and from being a member of the parliament if:

                                            • He is an undischarged insolvent
                                            • He holds an office of profits in the service of Pakistan
                                            • He has been convicted for any offence involving moral turpitude
                                            325

                                            A Prime Minister against whom a motion of no Confidence has been tabled can advise the President to dissolve the National Assembly?

                                            Correct Answer: No
                                            Show explanation
                                            Explanation

                                            The Constitution of Pakistan does not allow a Prime Minister, against whom a motion of no confidence has been tabled, to advise the President to dissolve the National Assembly.

                                            326

                                            According to the present form of Constitution the govt. can hold a referendum on any of national importance _____?

                                            Correct Answer: Yes
                                            Show explanation
                                            Explanation

                                            According to the present form of the Constitution of Pakistan, the government can hold a referendum on any matter of national importance as per Article 48(6).

                                            327

                                            The President of Pakistan can hold office for _____?

                                            Correct Answer: Two consecutive terms
                                            Show explanation
                                            Explanation

                                            According to the Constitution of Pakistan, the President can serve a maximum of two consecutive five-year terms.

                                            After completing these terms, they must step down and cannot immediately seek re-election.

                                            328

                                            The Constitution of Pakistan guarantees _____?

                                            Correct Answer: All of these
                                            Show explanation
                                            Explanation

                                            The Constitution of Pakistan guarantees:

                                            • Equality of citizens.
                                            • Preservation of language, script, and culture
                                            • Protection of property rights

                                            It ensuring fundamental rights for all citizens.

                                            329

                                            When National Assembly and Senate are referred together, they are termed as ______?

                                            Correct Answer: Majlis-e-Shoora (Parliament)
                                            Show explanation
                                            Explanation

                                            When the National Assembly and Senate of Pakistan are referred together, they are termed as Majlis-e-Shoora (Parliament).

                                            It is the formal name for Pakistan's legislative body.

                                            330

                                            The Constitution of Pakistan is comprised of how many parts?

                                            Correct Answer: XII
                                            Show explanation
                                            Explanation

                                            The Constitution of Pakistan is divided into 12 parts, which are:

                                            Part I: Preliminary

                                            Part II: Fundamental Rights and Principles of Policy

                                            Part III: The Federation of Pakistan

                                            Part IV: The Provinces

                                            Part V: Relations between Federation and Provinces

                                            Part VI: Financial Provisions

                                            Part VII: The Judicature

                                            Part VIII: Elections

                                            Part IX: Islamic Provisions

                                            Part X: Emergency Provisions

                                            Part XI: Amendment of Constitution

                                            Part XII: Miscellaneous and Transitional Provisions

                                            331

                                            As of 2021, how many amendments have been made to the constitution of Pakistan?

                                            Correct Answer: 26
                                            Show explanation
                                            Explanation

                                            25th Amendments     

                                            Merges Federally Administered Tribal Areas with Khyber Pakhtunkhwa.                     

                                            Enactment date 31 May 2018

                                            26th Amendments

                                            The seats of tribal districts in the National Assembly of Pakistan will be retained at 12 while their seats in the Khyber Pakhtunkhwa Assembly have been increased to 24 from 16.     

                                            Enactment date 13 May 2019

                                            332

                                            Out of following early problems of Pakistan; which one had no connection with India?

                                            Correct Answer: Language problem
                                            Show explanation
                                            Explanation

                                            The language problem in early Pakistan, primarily centered around the debate between Urdu and Bengali, had no direct connection with India.

                                            Unlike issues like the Kashmir dispute, division of assets, and the canal water dispute.

                                            333

                                            The song Mera Imaan Pakistan was sung by?

                                            Correct Answer: Nusrat Fateh Ali Khan
                                            Show explanation
                                            Explanation

                                            The song "Imaan Pakistan" was sung by Nusrat Fateh Ali Khan

                                            . This patriotic song was released in 1996 as part of a national campaign to promote unity and patriotism in Pakistan.

                                            334

                                            "Hindu Mahasaba" was _______?

                                            Correct Answer: An extremist Hindu organization
                                            Show explanation
                                            Explanation

                                            The Hindu Mahasabha was an extremist Hindu organization established in 1915.

                                            It promoting Hindu nationalism and opposing the political movements of Muslims in India.

                                            The Hindu Mahasabha is a political party in India.

                                            Founders: Vinayak Damodar Savarkar, Pandit Madan Mohan Malaviya, Lala Lajpat Rai

                                            335

                                            The book Chand Ham Asar was written by:

                                            Correct Answer: Moulvi Abul Haq
                                            Show explanation
                                            Explanation

                                            Moulvi Abul Haq authored "Chand Ham Asar," 

                                            He was first published in 1922.

                                            336

                                            Pakistan Resolution of 1940 was seconded by _____?

                                            Correct Answer: Chaudhry Khaliq-uz-Zaman
                                            Show explanation
                                            Explanation

                                            The Lahore Resolution is commonly known as the Pakistan Resolution.

                                            It was moved in the general session by A. K. Fazlul Huq, the Chief Minister of undivided Bengal.

                                            It was seconded by:

                                            • Chaudhry Khaliquzzaman from the United Provinces
                                            • Zafar Ali Khan from Punjab
                                            • Sardar Aurangzeb Khan from the North-West Frontier Province
                                            • Sir Abdullah Haroon from Sindh
                                            337

                                            Where did the incident of Jalian Wala Bagh tragedy take place?

                                            Correct Answer: Amritsar
                                            Show explanation
                                            Explanation

                                            Ø  The Jallianwala Bagh massacre took place on 13 April 1919,

                                            Ø  when Acting Brigadier-General Reginald Dyer ordered troops of the British Indian Army to fire into Indian civilians in Jallianwala Bagh, Amritsar, Punjab,

                                            Ø  In Jallianwala Bagh massacre killing at least 379 people and injuring over 1,200 other people
                                            338

                                            In 1905 which important event of Indian history took place?

                                            Correct Answer: Partition of Bengal
                                            Show explanation
                                            Explanation

                                            Lord Curzon divided Bengal into two provinces on 16-10-1905

                                            on October 16, 1905, by Lord Curzon, the British colonial administrator of India at the time

                                            The partition of Bengal was the most important event during the rule of Lord Curzon

                                            The province was divided into West Bengal, which had a majority of Hindus,

                                            and East Bengal, which had a majority of Muslims

                                            ****

                                            339

                                            A Colonel of Pakistan army is equal to which rank of Navy?

                                            Correct Answer: Captain
                                            Show explanation
                                            Explanation

                                            In the Pakistan Armed Forces, a Colonel in the Army is equivalent to a Captain in the Navy.

                                            Major (Army) to Lieutenant Commander (Navy).

                                            Brigadier (Army) to Commodore (Navy).

                                            340

                                            Which of the following served as a Caretaker Prime Minister of Pakistan?

                                            Correct Answer: Ghulam Mustafa Jatoi
                                            Show explanation
                                            Explanation

                                            List of Caretaker Prime Ministers of Pakistan

                                             

                                            Number   

                                            Name

                                            Duration

                                            1

                                            Ghulam Mustafa Jatoi

                                            6 Aug 1990 to 6 Nov 1990

                                            2

                                            Balakh Sher Mazari

                                            18 Apr 1993 to 26 May 1993

                                            3

                                            Moeenuddin Qureshi

                                            18 July to 19 October 1993

                                            4

                                            Malik Meraj Khalid

                                            6 Nov 1996 to 17 Feb 1997

                                            5

                                            Muhammad Mian Soomro

                                            16 Nov 2007 to 24 Mar 2008

                                            6

                                            Mir Hazar Khan Khoso

                                            25 March 2013 to 5 Jun 2013

                                            7

                                            Nasirul Mulk

                                            1 June 2018 to 18 August 2018

                                            8

                                            Anwaarul Haq Kakar

                                            12 August 2023 till today

                                            341

                                            Who was the second Chief Minister of Punjab?

                                            Correct Answer: Mian Mumtaz Daultana
                                            Show explanation
                                            Explanation

                                            List of Chief Ministers of Punjab from 1947 to 2022.

                                            No.
