                                            342

                                            Which is the highest Forex earner For Pakistan out of the following commodities?

                                            Correct Answer: Cotton
                                            Show explanation
                                            Explanation

                                            Cotton is the most important cash crop in Pakistan

                                            cotton products export account for 55 percent of all foreign exchange earnings of the country.

                                            ND28-4-2023



                                            343

                                            "My Life: A Fragment" is a book written by _____?

                                            Correct Answer: Maulana Muhammad Ali Jauhar
                                            Show explanation
                                            Explanation

                                            "My Life: A Fragment" is an autobiography written by Maulana Muhammad Ali Jauhar

                                            Publication date: 1942.

                                            344

                                            Islamia College Lahore was established in which year?

                                            Correct Answer: 1886
                                            Show explanation
                                            Explanation

                                            The Islamia College, Civil Lines was originally founded by the Arya Samaj as a school in 1866



                                            History:

                                            Government Islamia College Civil Lines, formerly called Dayanand Anglo Vedic College

                                            It was later renamed Dayanand Anglo Vedic (DAV) College after Hindu leader Dayananda Saraswati.

                                            The college was nationalized by the Zulfiqar Ali Bhutto regime in 1972. 

                                            345

                                            Usman Marwandi is the real name of which historical figure?

                                            Correct Answer: Lal Shahbaz Qalandar
                                            Show explanation
                                            Explanation

                                            Usman Marwandi is the real name of Lal Shahbaz Qalandar.

                                            Lal Shahbaz Qalandar wrote several books in Persian and Arabic

                                            Lal Shahbaz Qalandar was a Sufi saint and poet of present-day Pakistan and Afghanistan


                                            346

                                            In which war did Pakistan use Stinger missiles?

                                            Correct Answer: Soviet-Afghan War
                                            Show explanation
                                            Explanation

                                            Pakistan used Stinger missiles during the Soviet-Afghan War (1979-1989) 

                                            For the support of Afghan mujahideen fighters against Soviet forces

                                            347

                                            Ustad Daman was a poet of which language?

                                            Correct Answer: Punjabi
                                            Show explanation
                                            Explanation

                                            Ustad Daman was a prominent poet of the Punjabi language

                                            Born
                                            Chiragh Deen

                                            3 September 1911 
                                            Lahore, 
                                            Died3 December 1984 (aged 73)
                                            Lahore
                                            Resting placeBanghbanpura, Lahore
                                            Years active1926 – late 1970s
                                            348

                                            Pakistan's Constitution provides that children and women are not employed in vocations unsuited to their age or sex?

                                            Correct Answer: Correct
                                            Show explanation
                                            Explanation

                                            Pakistan's Constitution includes provisions, particularly in Article 11 and Article 37(e).

                                            It prohibit the employment of children and women in vocations unsuited to their age or sex.

                                            It ensuring protection against hazardous work conditions.

                                            349

                                            If a strike or lock-out is commenced without giving notice of conciliation to the other party of the dispute; it will be considered ______?

                                            Correct Answer: Illegal
                                            Show explanation
                                            Explanation

                                            A strike or lock-out that begins without giving notice of conciliation to the other party is considered illegal under labor laws.

                                            As proper procedures must be followed to ensure fair and lawful industrial actions.

                                            350

                                            A Collection Bargaining Agent (CBA) is a _______?

                                            Correct Answer: A registered trade union elected by secret ballot
                                            Show explanation
                                            Explanation

                                            A Collective Bargaining Agent (CBA) is a registered trade union that is elected by secret ballot.

                                            It represents the workers in negotiations with the employer on issues such as wages, working conditions, and other employment terms.

                                            351

                                            In a province, a Worker's Trade Union is registered with the _____?

                                            Correct Answer: Registrar Trade Unions
                                            Show explanation
                                            Explanation

                                            In a province, a Worker's Trade Union is registered with the Registrar of Trade Unions.

                                            The Registrar is responsible for maintaining records and overseeing the legal registration and compliance of trade unions within the province.

                                            352

                                            The Industrial Relations Ordinance, 2002 emphasis participation of workers in management of an establishment through ______?

                                            Correct Answer: All of them
                                            Show explanation
                                            Explanation

                                            The Industrial Relations Ordinance, 2002 emphasizes the participation of workers in the management of an establishment through various means.

                                            Including Workers Council, Management Committee, and Joint Management Board.

                                            353

                                            Which Article of the Constitution provides the freedom of association and the right to form unions?

                                            Correct Answer: 17
                                            Show explanation
                                            Explanation

                                            The freedom of association and the right to form unions are provided under Article 17 of the Constitution of Pakistan.

                                            This article guarantees the right to form and join unions or associations for all citizens.

                                            354

                                            Under the Factories Act, 1934 no worker can be required to work in any establishment in excess of _____?

                                            Correct Answer: 9 hours a day
                                            Show explanation
                                            Explanation

                                            Under the Factories Act, 1934, no worker can be required to work in any establishment for more than 9 hours a day.

                                            This regulation ensures that workers have regulated working hours and avoids excessive work conditions.

                                            355

                                            Under the Constitution, Labour is a ______?

                                            Correct Answer: Concurrent subject
                                            Show explanation
                                            Explanation

                                            Under the Constitution of Pakistan, labor is a concurrent subject.

                                            This means that both the federal and provincial governments can legislate on labor matters, with federal laws taking precedence in case of conflict.

                                            356

                                            Article 11 of the Pakistan's Constitution prohibits all forms of _____?

                                            Correct Answer: All of them
                                            Show explanation
                                            Explanation
                                            • Article 11 of the Constitution of Pakistan prohibits
                                            • Slavery: Forbidden in any form.
                                            • Forced Labour: Prohibited, including "begar" (forced unpaid labor).
                                            • Child Labor: Prohibited in hazardous environments for children under 14.
                                            357

                                            The 1st Muslim conquest in India took place in _____?

                                            Correct Answer: 712 AD
                                            Show explanation
                                            Explanation

                                            The 1st Muslim conquest in India occurred in 712 AD when:

                                            • Muhammad bin Qasim, a 17-year-old general from the Umayyad Caliphate.
                                            • Defeated Raja Dahir in the Battle of Aror.
                                            • Establishing Muslim rule in Sindh.
                                            358

                                            In Men's Hockey Champions Trophy 2014, Pakistan secured which position?

                                            Correct Answer: 2nd position
                                            Show explanation
                                            Explanation

                                            In the Men's Hockey Champions Trophy 2014, Pakistan secured 2nd position.

                                            They lost to Germany 2-0 in the finals.

                                            The tournament was held in Bhubaneswar, India, from December 6-14, 2014.

                                            359

                                            At the time of Independence (1947) was the biggest city of Pakistan (Population Wise)?

                                            Correct Answer: Karachi
                                            Show explanation
                                            Explanation

                                            At the time of the partition of India in 1947, Karachi was the largest in Sindh with an estimated population of 400,000 people


                                            On 15 August 1947 Capital of Sindh shifted from Karachi to Hyderabad and Karachi was made the national capital of Pakistan.

                                            Karachi is the largest city in Pakistan

                                            Karachi is the 12th largest in the world, with a population of over 20 million

                                            Karachi remained the capital of Pakistan from 1947 to 1959.

                                            The First Museum of Pakistan was established in Karachi on 17 April 1950

                                            ND28-4-2023


                                            360

                                            Who was the Captain of Pakistan Cricket team during World Cup 2015?

                                            Correct Answer: Misbah-ul-Haq
                                            Show explanation
                                            Explanation

                                            Pakistan's 15-man squad for the ICC Cricket World Cup

                                            Ahmed Shehzad, Ehsan Adil, Haris Sohail, Junaid Khan, Misbah-ul-Haq (capt), Mohammad Hafeez, Mohammad Irfan, Sarfraz Ahmed (wk), Shahid Afridi, Sohaib Maqsood, Sohail Khan, Umar Akmal, Wahab Riaz, Yasir Shah, Younus Khan.

                                            ****************

                                            VU FIA CBT  14-12-2021

                                            361

                                            Who was appointed Prime Minister after the imposition of Martial Law in 1958?

                                            Correct Answer: Ayub Khan
                                            Show explanation
                                            Explanation

                                            After the imposition of Martial Law in 1958, General Ayub Khan took control as the Chief Martial Law Administrator.

                                            He later appointed himself as the Prime Minister.

                                            He eventually became the President of Pakistan.


                                            Ø  Iskandar Ali Mirza who was the first president of Pakistan abrogated the constitution of 1956 on 07-October-1958.

                                            Ø  Ayoub Khan was made chief martial law after this abrogation.

                                            Ø  Feroz Khan Noun was the prime minister at the time of first Martial Law in Pakistan.

                                            ************

                                            List of Martial Laws Held in Pakistan from 1947 to 2022

                                            Sr#

                                             Martial Law Number

                                            Date of Martial Law

                                            Martial Law Implemented by

                                            1

                                            First Martial Law

                                            7th October 1958

                                            Iskandar Mirza

                                            2

                                            2nd Martial Law

                                            25th March 1969

                                            General Yahya Khan

                                            3

                                            3rd Martial Law

                                            25th July 1977

                                            General Zia ul Haq

                                            4

                                            4th Martial Law

                                            12th October 1999

                                            General Pervaiz Musharraf

                                             

                                            There are total four martial laws Implemented in Pakistan from 1947 to 2022

                                            ******

                                            ND30-12-2022

                                            362

                                            Who was the Foreign Minister in the Interim Government of British India formed before the partition?

                                            Correct Answer: Jawaharlal Nehru
                                            Show explanation
                                            Explanation

                                            Jawaharlal Nehru served as the Foreign Minister in the Interim Government of British India, formed in 1946 before the partition.

                                            He later became the first Prime Minister of independent India.

                                            363

                                            The founder of ‘Zamindar’ (newspaper) was ______?

                                            Correct Answer: Maulana Zafar Ali Khan
                                            Show explanation
                                            Explanation
                                            • The founder of ‘Zamindar’ (newspaper) was Maulana Zafar Ali Khan
                                            • ***
                                            • Zafar Ali Khan, was a Pakistani writer, poet, translator, and journalist who played an important role in the Pakistan Movement against the British Raj.
                                            • He is generally considered to be "the father of Urdu journalism." 
                                            • Born: 1873, Sialkot
                                            • Died: November 27, 1956, Wazirabad
                                            364

                                            Mr. Jinnah presented his 14-Points in March 1929, In Muslim League’s session held at _____?

                                            Correct Answer: Dehli
                                            Show explanation
                                            Explanation

                                            Jinnah gave his points in order to safeguard and protect the rights and interests of Muslims in March 1929.

                                            Muhammad Ali Johar helped Quaid e Azam in preparation for fourteen points 

                                            Nehru report presented in 1928

                                            Jinnah’s Fourteen Points presented in 1929

                                            1.      Federal constitution with residual powers with the provinces.

                                            2.      Provincial autonomy.

                                            3.      No constitutional amendment without the agreement of the states.

                                            4.      All legislatures and elected bodies to have adequate Muslim representation without reducing Muslim majority in a province to minority or equality.

                                            5.      Adequate Muslim representation of Muslims in the services and in self-governing bodies.

                                            6.      1/3rd representation of Muslims in the Central Legislature.

                                            7.      1/3rd Muslim members in the central and state cabinets.

                                            8.      Separate electorates.

                                            9.      No bill to be passed in any legislature if 3/4th of a minority community considers it against its interests.

                                            10.   Any reorganisation of territories not to affect the Muslim majority in Bengal, Punjab and the NWFP.

                                            11.   Separation of Sindh from Bombay Presidency.

                                            12.   Constitutional reforms in the NWFP and Baluchistan.

                                            13.   Full religious freedom for all communities.

                                            14.   Protection of the religious, cultural, educational and language rights of Muslims.

                                            ND23-4-2023

                                            365

                                            In 1954, Pakistan joined ____?

                                            Correct Answer: SEATO
                                            Show explanation
                                            Explanation
                                            • In 1954 Pakistan become a member of SEATO
                                            • **
                                            • SEATO is an abbreviation of: South East Asia Treaty Organization
                                            • **
                                            • SEATO was founded on 8th September 1954 and dissolved on 30th June 1977

                                            • In September of 1954,
                                            • the United States,
                                            • France,
                                            • Great Britain,
                                            • New Zealand,
                                            • Australia,
                                            • the Philippines,
                                            • Thailand
                                            • and Pakistan formed the Southeast Asia Treaty Organization,
                                            • or SEATO.

                                            • MUG 05-12-2022



                                            366

                                            The constitution reform introduced in British India in the year 1909 is generally referred as _____?

                                            Correct Answer: Minto-Morley Reforms
                                            Show explanation
                                            Explanation

                                            The Indian Councils Act 1909, commonly known as the Morley–Minto or Minto–Morley Reforms

                                            The Indian Councils Act 1909,  was an act of the Parliament of the United Kingdom

                                            The Indian Councils Act (1909) followed discussions between John Morley, Secretary of State for India (1905–14), and Lord Minto, viceroy (1905–10).

                                            ND28-4-2023



                                            367

                                            Who was the prime minister at the time of first Martial Law in Pakistan?

                                            Correct Answer: Feroz Khan Nuun
                                            Show explanation
                                            Explanation

                                            Ø  Iskandar Ali Mirza who was the first president of Pakistan abrogated the constitution of 1956 on 07-October-1958.

                                            Ø  Ayoub Khan was made chief martial law after this abrogation.

                                            Ø  Feroz Khan Noun was the prime minister at the time of first Martial Law in Pakistan.
                                            ************

                                            List of Martial Laws Held in Pakistan from 1947 to 2022

                                            Sr#

                                             Martial Law Number

                                            Date of Martial Law

                                            Martial Law Implemented by

                                            1

                                            First Martial Law

                                            7th October 1958

                                            Iskandar Mirza

                                            2

                                            2nd Martial Law

                                            25th March 1969

                                            General Yahya Khan

                                            3

                                            3rd Martial Law

                                            25th July 1977

                                            General Zia ul Haq

                                            4

                                            4th Martial Law

                                            12th October 1999

                                            General Pervaiz Musharraf

                                             

                                            There are total four martial laws Implemented in Pakistan from 1947 to 2022

                                            ******

                                            ND30-12-2022

                                            368

                                            Who was the Warren Hastings?

                                            Correct Answer: Governor General of India
                                            Show explanation
                                            Explanation

                                            Warren Hastings was governor general of Bengal from 1772 to 1785.

                                            Confused Questions

                                            First Governor of Bengal:

                                            Robert Clive

                                             

                                            first Governor-General of Bengal was

                                            Warren Hastings

                                             

                                            first Governor-General of India was

                                            William Bentinck

                                             

                                            Who was the first viceroy of India?

                                            Lord Canning

                                             

                                            Who was the last Viceroy of India?

                                            Lord Mountbatten

                                            369

                                            Boundary between India and China is called ________?

                                            Correct Answer: McMahon Line
                                            Show explanation
                                            Explanation

                                            The boundary line between India and China is called the McMohan Line

                                            This line was negotiated between Tibet and Great Britainat the End of the Shimla Conference

                                            The line was drawn between Oct 1913 and July 1914

                                            It was named after Sir Henry McMahon



                                            Boundaries
                                            		Between
                                            MacMahon lineChina and India
                                            Durand LinePakistan and Afghanistan 
                                            Radcliffe LineIndia and Pakistan
                                            24th ParallelIndia and Pakistan
                                            Maginot LineGermany and France
                                            Mannerheim LineRussia and Finland
                                            Oder-Neisse LinePoland and Germany


                                            370

                                            The time during which the workers employed are at the disposal of employer excluding any interval allowed for rest and meals is called _____?

                                            Correct Answer: Hours of work
                                            Show explanation
                                            Explanation

                                            The time during which workers are at the disposal of the employer, excluding intervals for rest and meals, is called "Hours of work."

                                            It represents the total time employees are actively engaged in their duties.

                                            371

                                            A learner who is paid an allowance during the period of training is termed as _____?

                                            Correct Answer: Probationer
                                            Show explanation
                                            Explanation

                                            A probationer is a person undergoing a trial period in a new job or role, often receiving a stipend or allowance.

                                            This period tests their suitability for permanent employment.

                                            372

                                            To which officer the application for establishing a union is submitted as per law?

                                            Correct Answer: Registrar
                                            Show explanation
                                            Explanation

                                            The application for establishing a union is submitted to the Registrar of Trade Unions as per the law.

                                            The Registrar is responsible for overseeing the registration and regulation of trade unions.

                                            373

                                            Minimum, how many workers of an establishment are required to form a Union under the law?

                                            Correct Answer: 50
                                            Show explanation
                                            Explanation

                                            Under the law, a minimum of 50 workers from an establishment are required to form a union.

                                            This threshold ensures that the union represents a significant portion of the workforce.

                                            374

                                            A strike declared, commenced or continued other than is accordance with the provisions of the Punjab Industrial Relations Act, 2010 is an _____?

                                            Correct Answer: Illegal strike
                                            Show explanation
                                            Explanation

                                            A strike that is declared, commenced, or continued not in accordance with the provisions of the Punjab Industrial Relations Act, 2010, is considered an illegal strike.

                                            Such strikes are not protected by the law and can lead to legal consequences.

                                            375

                                            The Punjab Industrial Relations Act; 2010 extends to the ______?

                                            Correct Answer: Whole of the Punjab
                                            Show explanation
                                            Explanation

                                            The Punjab Industrial Relations Act, 2010, extends to the entire province of Punjab.

                                            It governs labor relations and industrial disputes across all industrial units and workplaces within the province.

                                            376

                                            In extra-ordinary circumstances, an employee is bound to inform his employer regarding his absence (to obtain casual leave) within ____?

                                            Correct Answer: 3 days
                                            Show explanation
                                            Explanation

                                            In extraordinary circumstances, an employee is generally required to inform their employer about their absence and request casual leave within 3 days.

                                            This ensures that the employer is notified in a timely manner.

                                            377

                                            In Punjab, two Labour Appellate Tribunals are established at _____?

                                            Correct Answer: Lahore and Multan
                                            Show explanation
                                            Explanation

                                            In Punjab, two Labour Appellate Tribunals are established in Lahore and Multan.

                                            These tribunals handle appeals related to labor disputes and employment issues.

                                            378

                                            Pakistan's share in global exports is _____?

                                            Correct Answer: 0.18%
                                            Show explanation
                                            Explanation

                                            Pakistan's share in global exports is about 0.18%.

                                            It indicating its modest contribution to the overall global trade market.

                                            379

                                            Out of the following, who has not been Foreign Minister of Pakistan?

                                            Correct Answer: Chaudhry Muhammad Ali
                                            Show explanation
                                            Explanation

                                            Chaudhry Muhammad Ali was the 4th Prime Minister of Pakistan (1955-1956), but he has not held the office of Foreign Minister.

                                            The other have all served as Foreign Minister of Pakistan:

                                            • Muhammad Ali Bogra (1954-1955)
                                            • Mian Arshad Hussain (1968-1969)
                                            • Aziz Ahmed (1977-1977)
                                            380

                                            Which book out of the following has been written by Mr. Zulifqar Ali Bhutto?

                                            Correct Answer: The Great Tragedy
                                            Show explanation
                                            Explanation

                                            The Great Tragedy is written by Z.A Bhutto 

                                            This book was Published in 1971

                                            Additional information:

                                            Zulfikar Ali Bhutto was the former PM and President of Pakistan 

                                            And founder of the Pakistan People's Party.

                                            381

                                            First Pakistani writer who represented Pakistan in UNO?

                                            Correct Answer: Ahmed Shah Bukhari Patras
                                            Show explanation
                                            Explanation
                                            • The first Pakistani writer who represented Pakistan in the UNO is Ahmed Shah Bukhari Patras.
                                            • Pakistan joined the United Nations on September 30, 1947, shortly after its independence on August 14, 1947.
                                            • The Permanent Representative of Pakistan to the United Nations is Pakistan's diplomatic representative to the United Nations.
                                            382

                                            Russia withdrew from Afghanistan for the ___?

                                            Correct Answer: Geneva Accord
                                            Show explanation
                                            Explanation

                                            The final and complete withdrawal of the 40th Army (Soviet Union) from Afghanistan began on 15 May 1988 and ended on 15 February 1989

                                            under the leadership of Colonel-General Boris Gromov.

                                            The Soviet military had been one of the main combatants in the Soviet–Afghan War since its beginning in 1979.

                                            ND4-5-2023

                                            383

                                            "Rabi Crops" are sown in _____?

                                            Correct Answer: October/November
                                            Show explanation
                                            Explanation

                                            "Rabi crops" are sown in October/November during the spring season.

                                            They are harvested in March/April during the winter season.

                                            These crops include wheat, barley, and mustard.

                                            384

                                            Ms. Yasmin Lari, a recipient of Sitara-e-Imtiaz, is known for her contributions to which field?

                                            Correct Answer: Architecture
                                            Show explanation
                                            Explanation

                                            Yasmeen Lari, Pakistan's first female architect, has been awarded the prestigious Royal Gold Medal for Architecture.

                                            She is also a recipient of the Sitara-e-Imtiaz for her significant contributions to the field.

                                            385

                                            How many times Pakistan won the "Blind Cricket World Cup"?

                                            Correct Answer: 2
                                            Show explanation
                                            Explanation

                                            Pakistan has won the Blind Cricket World Cup twice, in 1998 and 2002.

                                            386

                                            When the first General Elections under the 1973 constitution were held?

                                            Correct Answer: 1977
                                            Show explanation
                                            Explanation

                                            The first General Elections under the 1973 constitution of Pakistan were held in 1977.

                                            The elections were held on March 7, 1977.

                                            They were won by the Pakistan Peoples Party (PPP) led by Zulfikar Ali Bhutto.

                                            387

                                            Which Act was adopted as Pakistan's first constitution in 1947?

                                            Correct Answer: Government of India Act 1935
                                            Show explanation
                                            Explanation

                                            Government of India Act 1935 adopted as Pakistan's first constitution in 1947.

                                            The Government of India Act was a total failure as it was not able to give what it had proposed.

                                            Act of 1935 consisted of 321 sections

                                            The concept of dyarchy that was brought proved to be wrong and also the act was opposed by the Indian National Congress and the Muslim League.



                                            388

                                            In April 2017, Noble Laureate Pakistani Malala Yousaf Zai was designed as ____?

                                            Correct Answer: UN Messenger of Peace
                                            Show explanation
                                            Explanation

                                            In April 2017, Malala Yousafzai, the youngest Nobel Laureate, was designated as a UN Messenger of Peace.

                                            By the United Nations for her work advocating for girls' education worldwide.

                                            389

                                            Hub Dam is in?

                                            Correct Answer: Karachi
                                            Show explanation
                                            Explanation

                                            It is situated 56 km from Karachi city in Karachi and Lasbela Districts in Sindh and Balochistan 

                                            Hub Dam is a reservoir on the Hub River.

                                            Construction began in September 1963 

                                            The opening date on June 1981


                                            390

                                            Rafique Tarar had given his resignation to the?

                                            Correct Answer: Gen Pervez Musharraf
                                            Show explanation
                                            Explanation

                                            Rafique Tarar, the former President of Pakistan, resigned during Pervez Musharraf's presidency.


                                            History

                                            Muhammad Rafiq Tarar served as the ninth president of Pakistan 

                                            The presidential term of Muhammad Rafiq Tarar: January 1, 1998 – June 20, 2001

                                            He was the Member of the Senate of Pakistan from (1997–1998)

                                            He served as a senior justice of the Supreme Court of Pakistan from 1991 to 1994

                                            He was the 28th Chief Justice of Lahore High Court from 1989 to 1991

                                            He  was born on November 1929 – died on 7 March 2022

                                            391

                                            Syed Murad Ali Shah has became Chef Minister of Sindh for ?

                                            Correct Answer: Third
                                            Show explanation
                                            Explanation

                                            In 2024. Syed Murad Ali Shah elected chief minister of Sindh for the third time in a row

                                            Total votes: 168 votes

                                            Obtained votes: 112 votes

                                            392

                                            Which Caretaker Prime Minister of Pakistan addressed the 78th session of the United Nation?

                                            Correct Answer: Anwaar-ul-Haq Kakar
                                            Show explanation
                                            Explanation

                                            On 22 September 2023, Anwaar-ul-Haq Kakar became the first caretaker prime minister  of Pakistan to address  78th session of United Nations General Assembly (UNGA) in New York, USA

                                            393

                                            Muhammad Ali Johar studied in ______?

                                            Correct Answer: Aligarh University
                                            Show explanation
                                            Explanation

                                            Maulana Muhammad Ali Jauhar studied at Aligarh Muslim University (AMU).

                                            It was originally known as Muhammadan Anglo-Oriental College.

                                            It was founded by Sir Syed Ahmad Khan in 1875 and became AMU in 1920.

                                            394

                                            The joint military training exercise ‘Druzhba - III’ has started between following countries?

                                            Correct Answer: Russia and Pakistan
                                            Show explanation
                                            Explanation

                                            The two week long Durzbha-V was conducted at Tarbela and NCTC Pabbi.

                                            The first-ever joint exercise between Pakistan and the Russian military took place in 2016.

                                            395

                                            Titu Mir worked for the Muslim peasant of _____?

                                            Correct Answer: Bengal
                                            Show explanation
                                            Explanation

                                            Bengal is where Titu Mir worked for the Muslim peasants.

                                            He led a movement against oppressive landlords and British colonial authorities in the early 19th century.

                                            Titu Mir is also known as Syed Mir Nisar Ali.

                                            396

                                            Who was the first Governor General of India?

                                            Correct Answer: Hasting
                                            Show explanation
                                            Explanation
                                            • Warren Hastings was the first Governor General of India, serving from 1773 to 1785.
                                              • Lord Canning was the last Governor General, serving until 1858 when the British crown took direct control.
                                              397

                                              Which famous newspaper of Pakistan also publishes from UK?

                                              Correct Answer: Jang
                                              Show explanation
                                              Explanation

                                              Jang is the famous Pakistani newspaper that also publishes from the UK.

                                              It is one of Pakistan's largest Urdu-language newspapers with international editions.

                                              398

                                              In which city of Pakistan most COVID-19 (Corona) cases are reported?

                                              Correct Answer: Karachi
                                              Show explanation
                                              Explanation

                                              As of May 7, 2021, Karachi recorded about 189,000 confirmed COVID-19 cases, comprising 22% of Pakistan's total.

                                              Lahore, as of September 5, 2020, reported 170,000 cases, making up 19%.

                                              Islamabad and Peshawar recorded 79,000 and 47,000 cases respectively

                                              399

                                              Which river is not located in Balochistan?

                                              Correct Answer: Shyok
                                              Show explanation
                                              Explanation

                                              Shyok is located in Gilgit Baltistan

                                              while

                                              24 rivers are present in Pakistan

                                              5 rivers flow in Punjab Province

                                              4 rivers flow in Sindh Province

                                              7 rivers flow in Balochistan Province

                                              8 rivers flow in Khyber Pakhtunkhwa Province

                                              400

                                              Who was the first Governor-General of the Dominion of India?

                                              Correct Answer: Lord Mountbatten
                                              Show explanation
                                              Explanation

                                              ü  The first official Governor-General of British India was Lord William Bentinck,

                                              ü  The first Governor-General of the Dominion of India was Lord Mountbatten.

                                              401

                                              Who was the first female Governor of the Sindh province?

                                              Correct Answer: Begum Ra'ana Liaquat Ali
                                              Show explanation
                                              Explanation
                                              • Begum Ra'ana Liaquat Ali Khan was the first woman governor of Pakistan.
                                              • She served as the governor of Sindh from February 1973 to February 1976.
                                              402

                                              Muhammad Rafiq Tarar was elected as president of Pakistan in which year___?

                                              Correct Answer: 1998
                                              Show explanation
                                              Explanation

                                              He belongs to Pakistan Muslim league

                                              Nawaz sharif was PM in 1998.

                                              Pervaiz Musharraf was chief executive

                                              Rafiaq Tarar tenure 1998-2001.

                                              403

                                              On 19 March 2008, the National Assembly elected its first woman speaker _____?

                                              Correct Answer: Fahameeda Mirza
                                              Show explanation
                                              Explanation

                                              Fahmeeda Mirza served as the 18th and only women Speaker of the National Assembly of Pakistan from March 2008 to June 2013.

                                              404

                                              The Shaheed Benazir Bhutto University is located in which province?

                                              Correct Answer: Sindh
                                              Show explanation
                                              Explanation

                                              The Shaheed Benazir Bhutto University is located in Sindh.

                                              SBBU, Shaheed Benazirabad is a public sector university in Nawabshah.

                                              It was established in 2012.

                                              405

                                              Which sector has received significant investment through the CPEC initiative?

                                              Correct Answer: Power Generation
                                              Show explanation
                                              Explanation

                                              CPEC has invested significantly in power generation.

                                              Approximately $33 billion has been allocated to enhance Pakistan's energy infrastructure.

                                              406

                                              Operation Zarb-e-Azab was primarily aimed at combating _____?

                                              Correct Answer: Insurgency and terrorism
                                              Show explanation
                                              Explanation

                                              Operation Zarb-e-Azb, launched on June 15, 2014, by Army Chief General Raheel Sharif.

                                              It targeted insurgency and terrorism in North Waziristan and other regions, including FATA, Karachi, Balochistan, and Punjab.

                                              The operation concluded on February 22, 2017.

                                              407

                                              Which Constitutional amendment was made to extend the tenure of local government bodies?

                                              Correct Answer: 18th amendment
                                              Show explanation
                                              Explanation

                                              The 18th Amendment was enacted in 2010.

                                              It extended the tenure of local government bodies from three to five years.

                                              408

                                              Which of the following sectors received significant attention during the "CPEC" initiative?

                                              Correct Answer: Transportation infrastructure
                                              Show explanation
                                              Explanation

                                              CPEC focused on developing transportation infrastructure.

                                              This includes roads, railways, and ports to improve connectivity between China and Pakistan.

                                              The China-Pakistan Economic Corridor (CPEC) is a series of energy and infrastructure projects, some of which connect China’s Xinjiang to Gwadar.

                                              Initiated in mid-2013 and formally launched in April 2015.

                                              CPEC is generally described as a $46 billion package of Chinese aid.

                                              409

                                              Which Political Party was founded by Zulifqar Ali Bhutto in 1967?

                                              Correct Answer: Pakistan People's Party (PPP)
                                              Show explanation
                                              Explanation

                                              Pakistan People's Party (PPP) was founded by Zulfikar Ali Bhutto.

                                              Established on November 30, 1967.

                                              410

                                              Which area is called gun factory of the tribal areas?

                                              Correct Answer: Darra Adam Khel
                                              Show explanation
                                              Explanation

                                              Darra Adam Khel, located near Kohat in Khyber Pakhtunkhwa.

                                              It is famously known as the "gun factory" of the tribal areas.

                                              Due to its local production of firearms.

                                              Correct Answer: Abdul Qayyum Khan
                                              Show explanation
