                                              412

                                              Which pass connects Mingora city with Buner?

                                              Correct Answer: Karakar
                                              Show explanation
                                              Explanation

                                              Karakar pass is located in: Hindu Kush, KPK

                                               Important Passes of Pakistan

                                              1. Khyber Pass 

                                              connects: Peshawar to Kabul

                                              connects: Pakistan to Afghanistan

                                              Height 1070 meters, 3510 feet 


                                              2. Bolan Pass 

                                              Connects: JCB and Sibi with Quetta 

                                              Height: 1793 meters, 5884 feet 


                                              3. Lowari Pass 

                                              Connects: Chitral with Dir 

                                              Height: 3118 meters, 10230 feet 


                                              4. Broghil Pass 

                                              Location: Pakistan & Afghanistan border 

                                              Height: 3798 meters, 12460 feet 


                                              5. Malakand Pass 

                                              Connects: Dir with Chitral 

                                              Height: 1362 meters, 4468 feet 


                                              6. Shandur pass

                                              Connects: Gupis/Gilgit with Chitral 

                                              Height: 3700 meters, 12139 feet 


                                              7. Khanjerab Pass (top most in Pakistan) 

                                              Other name: Friendship Gate

                                              Connects: China with Pakistan 

                                              Height: 4693 meters, 15397 feet 


                                              8. Dorah pass

                                              Connects: Badakhshan province of Afghanistan with Chitral 

                                              Height: 4300 meters, 14108 feet 


                                              9. Babusar Pass 

                                              Connects: Mansehra/Abbotabad with Gilgit


                                              10. Kohat Pass

                                              Connects: Kohat with Peshawar 


                                              11. Khojak Pass

                                              Connects: Qilla Abdullah with Chaman


                                              12. Tochi Pass

                                              Connects: Ghazni with Bannu


                                              13. Karakar Pass

                                              Connects: Mingora with Buner 


                                              413

                                              KPK is located on which side of Pakistan?

                                              Correct Answer: North-West
                                              Show explanation
                                              Explanation
                                              KPK is a province of Pakistan.

                                              Located in the northwestern region

                                              KPK has 36 districts and 8th divisions.

                                              Khyber Pakhtunkhwa is the fourth-largest province of Pakistan by land area

                                              and the third-largest province by population. 

                                              The local languages of KPK are? Hindko, Pashto (major), Chirtali, Hindo (major)

                                              What is the most famous dish of KPK? Chapal kabab

                                              The Largest District of KPK by Area of Chitral is 57 km²

                                              Total Area: 39,282 mi²

                                              What is the total forest area of KPK? 1.21 million hectares

                                              Capital and largest city: Peshawar ( sixth-largest in Pakistan.)

                                              Established (as NWFP): 1 July 1970

                                              NWFP became KPK on 15 April 2010 after: 18th amendment

                                              The old name of KPK was NWFP

                                               NWFP become KPK on 15 April 2010

                                              The province of Khyber Pakhtunkhwa (KPK) after the FATA Merger consists of 35 districts and 7th divisions.
                                              ND13-5-2023

                                              414

                                              On the northern western part, Khyber Pakhtunkhwa has common border with ____?

                                              Correct Answer: Afghanistan
                                              Show explanation
                                              Explanation

                                              Khyber Pakhtunkhwa, a province in Pakistan, shares its northwestern border with Afghanistan, separated by the Durand Line.

                                              It is bounded by:

                                              • Afghanistan to the west and north.
                                              • Azad Kashmir and Gilgit-Baltistan to the east and northeast.
                                              • Punjab province to the southeast.
                                              • Balochistan province to the southwest.
                                              415

                                              Who conceived the idea of Pakistan?

                                              Correct Answer: Chowdhary Rahmat Ali
                                              Show explanation
                                              Explanation

                                              Chaudhary Rahmat Ali The man who conceived the idea of Pakistan".

                                              Choudhry Rahmat Ali

                                              چودھری رحمت علی

                                              Born16 November 1897
                                              Garhshankar, Punjab, British India

                                              Died3 February 1951 (aged 53)
                                              Cambridge, England

                                              Nationality  British Pakistani
                                              Academic background

                                              Notable works"Pakistan Declaration"
                                              Notable ideasConception of "Pakistan"
                                              416

                                              The tomb of Mughal Emperor Jahangir is situated in which city?

                                              Correct Answer: Lahore
                                              Show explanation
                                              Explanation

                                              The Tomb of Jahangir is a 17th-century mausoleum built for the Mughal Emperor Jahangir. 

                                              Opened: 1627

                                              Burials: Jahangir

                                              Completion date: 1637



                                              417

                                              Pound Sterling is the currency of which country?

                                              Correct Answer: England
                                              Show explanation
                                              Explanation

                                              The capital of England is London

                                              The Dialing code of England is +44

                                              The Currency of England is Pound sterling

                                              Cricket is national game of England 

                                              418

                                              According to 1973 constitution, who elects the president of Pakistan?

                                              Correct Answer: All of the above
                                              Show explanation
                                              Explanation

                                              Zulifqar Ali Bhutto introduced 1973 constitution in Pakistan on August 14, 1973.

                                              According to 1973 constitution, Provincial assembly, National assembly, and senate elect the President.

                                              Whereas the prime minister is elect by only national assembly.

                                              Bicameral Legislature system was introduced for the first time in Pakistan in the constitution of 1973.
                                              419

                                              Pakistan won gold medal in Olympics in which year?

                                              Correct Answer: All of these
                                              Show explanation
                                              Explanation

                                              Pakistan won its first Olympic Gold Medal in the field of Hockey in Rome held in 1960.

                                              Pakistan won its second Olympic Gold Medal in the field of Hockey in Mexico held in 1968.

                                              Pakistan won its third Olympic Gold Medal in in the field of Hockey Loss Angels, USA held in 1984.

                                              Pakistan won its fourth Olympic Gold Medal in the field of Javelin Throw in Paris held in 2024.

                                               *****

                                              420

                                              Who is the winner of first Hockey World Cup played in 1971?

                                              Correct Answer: Pakistan
                                              Show explanation
                                              Explanation

                                              On 2 August 1948, Pakistan national team, led by Ali Iqtidar Shah Dara, officially went on to play their first international game and tournament against Belgium winning the game 2–0 at the 1948 London Olympics.

                                              Pakistan is the most successful national field hockey team in the Hockey World Cup.

                                               Pakistanis the four-time champion: 

                                              1971

                                              1978

                                              1982

                                              1994.

                                              ***

                                              Pakistan defeats Spain in the 1971 hockey world cup.

                                              Pakistan beat India to secure the bronze medal at Hockey Champions Trophy 2012.


                                              Pakistan played its first International Hockey matches in 1948

                                              In 1994 year, Pakistan was the world champion of Hockey

                                              In 1994 en Pakistan won the World Hockey Cup last time


                                              421

                                              Which Pakistani athlete won gold medal in Taekwondo Championship 2024?

                                              Correct Answer: Aamir Khan
                                              Show explanation
                                              Explanation

                                              Pakistani athlete Aamir Khan won gold medal in Taekwondo Championship 2024.

                                              Aamir Khan belongs to Mingora, Swat.

                                              **

                                              About Taekwondo Championship 2024 (Martial Art)

                                              Edition: 7th

                                              Venue: Bangkok, Thailand

                                              Starting date: 10 Aug

                                              Closing date: 12 Aug

                                              **** 

                                              422

                                              Aligarh School was founded by?

                                              Correct Answer: Sir Syed Ahmed Khan
                                              Show explanation
                                              Explanation

                                              Sir Syed Ahmed Khan started Ali Garh School in 1875

                                              Ali Garh school converted into College 1877

                                              Ali Garh College was converted into a University in 1920


                                              Additional information:

                                              M.A.O college was to provide education to Muslims & Non-Muslims

                                              Henry Siddons was the headmaster of the M.A.O school

                                              Lord Lytton inaugurated M.A.O College in 1877

                                              In MAO Intermediate classes were started in 1878

                                              in 1881 B.A. degree classes were added.



                                              423

                                              Which are the two hottest provinces of Pakistan?

                                              Correct Answer: Sindh and Balochistan
                                              Show explanation
                                              Explanation

                                              Sindh and Balochistan are the two hottest provinces in Pakistan.

                                              In cities like Jacobabad and Sibi, temperatures can often exceed 50°C during summer.

                                              424

                                              Which age limit is prohibited for the employment of children according to Article 11(3) of the Constitution of Pakistan?

                                              Correct Answer: 14 years
                                              Show explanation
                                              Explanation

                                              According to Article 11(3) of the Constitution of Pakistan, the employment of children below the age of 14 years is prohibited.

                                              This provision aims to protect children from labor exploitation and ensure their right to education and development.

                                              425

                                              What was the significance of the Simla Agreement?

                                              Correct Answer: It was an agreement between India and Pakistan to respect the Line of Control.
                                              Show explanation
                                              Explanation

                                              The Simla Agreement was signed on July 2, 1972, between India and Pakistan.


                                              The agreement aimed to establish principles for the relationship between the two countries, including a commitment to respect the Line of Control (LoC) in Jammu and Kashmir.


                                              Additional information 

                                              Simla Conference Presided by Lord Wavell

                                              The Simla Conference of 1945 was a meeting between:

                                              the Viceroy of India Lord Wavell and the major political leaders of British India

                                              at the Viceregal Lodge in Simla

                                              **

                                              35 Muslim members participated in the Shimla conferences

                                              The Shimla Agreement was signed in 1972

                                              The Simla Agreement was signed by Prime Minister Indira Gandhi and President Zulfikar Ali Bhutto of Pakistan.

                                              426

                                              When did the first Indo-Pak war take place?

                                              Correct Answer: 1947-48
                                              Show explanation
                                              Explanation

                                              The Indo-Pakistani War of 1947–1948, also known as the First Kashmir War, was a war fought between India and Pakistan 

                                              It was the first of four Indo-Pakistani wars


                                              War between Pakistan and India
                                              1 Indo-Pakistani War of                                               1947
                                              2 Indo-Pakistani War of                                               1965
                                              3 Indo-Pakistani War of                                               1971
                                              4 Indo-Pakistani War of                                               1999

                                              427

                                              Who was the famous Indian leader known for his role in the independence movement and philosophy of non-violence?

                                              Correct Answer: Bhagat Singh
                                              Show explanation
                                              Explanation

                                              Gandhi's commitment to non-violence and justice inspired movements for civil rights and freedom across the world.

                                              Gandhi understood nonviolence from its Sanskrit root “Ahimsa”.








                                              428

                                              Which movement was launched by Allama Iqbal in 1930?

                                              Correct Answer: Pakistan Movement
                                              Show explanation
                                              Explanation

                                              Sir Muhammad Iqbal's 1930 Presidential Address to the 25th Session of the All-India Muslim League Allahabad, 29 December 1930.

                                              He proposed the idea of a separate nation for Muslims, which eventually led to the formulation of the Pakistan Movement


                                              Allama Iqbal is a National Poet of Pakistan

                                              Allama Iqbal Born: November 9, 1877, Sialkot

                                              Allama Iqbal Died: April 21, 1938, in Lahore

                                              Allama Iqbal Place of burial: Tomb of Allama Iqbal, Lahore

                                              429

                                              When Urdu Defence Association was founded?

                                              Correct Answer: August 1900
                                              Show explanation
                                              Explanation

                                              The Urdu Defence Association was an organization developed by Mohsin-ul-Mulk

                                              The Urdu Defence Association was started in April 1900

                                              Urdu Defence Association was founded In August 1900 In Luckhnow  City.

                                              ***

                                              The Urdu Defence Association started the advocacy for Urdu as the lingua franca of the Muslim community of India. 

                                              The association is regarded as an offshoot of the Aligarh Movement.

                                              430

                                              What rank did Pakistan secure in the Summer Olympic Games 2024 medal tally?

                                              Correct Answer: 62nd
                                              Show explanation
                                              Explanation

                                              Pakistan secured 62nd ranking in the Summer Olympic Games 2024.

                                              Pakistan won Single Gold Medal in Javelin Throw category.

                                              USA secured first position by winning a total of 126 medals. 

                                              Olympic Games 2024 held in Paris, France from 26 July to 11 Aug 2024.

                                              ****

                                              431

                                              How many gold medals has Pakistan won in the Olympic Games held in Paris, France in 2024?

                                              Correct Answer: One
                                              Show explanation
                                              Explanation

                                              Pakistan secured 62nd ranking in the Summer Olympic Games 2024.

                                              Pakistan won Single Gold Medal in Javelin Throw category.

                                              USA secured first position by winning a total of 126 medals. 

                                              Olympic Games 2024 held in Paris, France from 26 July to 11 Aug 2024.

                                              ****

                                              432

                                              Who is the flag bearer of Pakistan in the opening ceremony of 2024 Summer Olympic Games held in Paris?

                                              Correct Answer: Both A & B
                                              Show explanation
                                              Explanation

                                              Arhdad Nadeem & Jehanara Nabi were the flag bearer for Pakistan at the Olympic Games Opening Ceremony

                                              Pakistan secured 62nd ranking in the Summer Olympic Games 2024.

                                              Pakistan won Single Gold Medal in Javelin Throw category.

                                              USA secured first position by winning a total of 126 medals. 

                                              Olympic Games 2024 held in Paris, France from 26 July to 11 Aug 2024.

                                              433

                                              In which book of Hafiz is the national anthem of Pakistan written?

                                              Correct Answer: Chiragh Sahar
                                              Show explanation
                                              Explanation

                                              The National Anthem of Pakistan is written in Chiragh Sahar the Book of Hafez Jalandhari.


                                              About National Anthem of Pakistan:

                                              • Written by: Hafeez Jalandhari in 1952
                                              • Music by: Ahmad G. Chagla in 1949
                                              • Adopted on: August 13, 1954
                                              • First recorded by: Ahmed Rushdi in 1954
                                              • Re-recorded after 68 years
                                              Key Facts:
                                              • 15 lines in the National Anthem of Pakistan
                                              • 48 words in the National Anthem of Pakistan
                                              • First played in front of Raza Shah Rahalvi of Iran
                                              • "Jan-i-Istaqbal" means Symbol of ambitions
                                              • First played on national radio on August 14, 1947
                                              • Duration of the National Anthem of Pakistan: 80 seconds (1 minute 20 seconds)
                                              • Pakistan's National Anthem is written in: Persian


                                              434

                                              Who wrote the first book on public administration?

                                              Correct Answer: Woodrow Wilson
                                              Show explanation
                                              Explanation

                                              The first book in the field of public administration is "The Study of Administration" by Woodrow Wilson.

                                              It was published in 1887

                                              It was based on an essay Wilson wrote 

                                              435

                                              How many total districts are there in Pakistan?

                                              Correct Answer: 170
                                              Show explanation
                                              Explanation

                                              There are a total of 170 districts in Pakistan.

                                              • Punjab: 42 districts
                                              • KPK: 38 districts
                                              • Balochistan: 36 districts
                                              • Sindh: 30 districts
                                              • Gilgit-Baltistan: 14 districts
                                              • Azad Kashmir: 10 districts

                                              Last verified on 03-06-2026

                                              436

                                              Who remained the Governor General and also the Prime Minister of Pakistan?

                                              Correct Answer: Khawaja Nazimuddin
                                              Show explanation
                                              Explanation

                                              Khawaja Nazimuddin served as both the Governor-General and Prime Minister of Pakistan.

                                              He was Governor-General from 14-09-1948 To 16-10-1951.

                                              He was Prime Minister from 17-10-1951 To 17-04-1953.

                                              437

                                              Collectively how many Governor Generals ruled over Pakistan?

                                              Correct Answer: 4
                                              Show explanation
                                              Explanation
                                              Governor-General of Pakistan

                                              #

                                              Name

                                              Date of Birth and Death

                                              1

                                              Muhammad Ali Jinnah

                                              25 December 1876 – 11 September 1948

                                              2

                                              Sir Khawaja Nazimuddin

                                              19 July 1894 - 22 October 1964

                                              3

                                              Ghulam Mohammad

                                              20 April 1895 - 12 September 1956

                                              4

                                              Iskander Mirza

                                              13 November 1899 - 12 November 1969
                                              438

                                              Arshad Nadeem is the famous player associated with which sport?

                                              Correct Answer: Javelin
                                              Show explanation
                                              Explanation

                                              Arshad Nadeem is a renowned athlete associated with the sport of javelin.

                                              He is known for his achievements in javelin throwing.

                                              He is including representing Pakistan in international competitions.

                                              439

                                              The Objective Resolution was passed on March 12th _____?

                                              Correct Answer: 1949
                                              Show explanation
                                              Explanation

                                              The Objectives Resolution was adopted by the Constituent Assembly of Pakistan

                                              It was founded on March 12, 1949

                                              Prime Minister, Liaquat Ali Khan presented it in the assembly on March 7, 1949.

                                              Out of 75 members of the assembly, 21 voted for it

                                              440

                                              River _____ flows between Pakistan and India as a trans-boundary river.

                                              Correct Answer: Ravi
                                              Show explanation
                                              Explanation

                                              The Ravi River is a transboundary river flowing through northwestern India and eastern Pakistan.

                                              Under the Indus Waters Treaty of 1960, its waters, along with those of the Sutlej and Beas rivers, are allocated to India.

                                              441

                                              Through Gwadar Port, ______ can be, monitored.

                                              Correct Answer: Strait of Hormuz
                                              Show explanation
                                              Explanation

                                              Through Gwadar Port, the Strait of Hormuz can be monitored.

                                              This strategic chokepoint is crucial for international maritime trade.

                                              This especially for oil shipments.

                                              442

                                              In 1993, when Ghulam Ishaq Khan was President, who was the Prime Minister of Pakistan?

                                              Correct Answer: Nawaz Sharif
                                              Show explanation
                                              Explanation

                                              In 1993, when Ghulam Ishaq Khan was President, Nawaz Sharif was the Prime Minister of Pakistan

                                              He served as 7th President of Pakistan

                                              In office: 17 August 1988 – 18 July 1993


                                              On 29th May 1988, General Zia-ul-Haq dismissed the government of Muhammad Khan Junejo to extend military rule.


                                              443

                                              On 1st October 1906, a Muslim political delegation led by whom demanded a separate electorate for Muslims?

                                              Correct Answer: Aga Khan
                                              Show explanation
                                              Explanation

                                              on 1st October 1906, a Muslim political delegation led by Aga Khan


                                              Met with the Viceroy of India to demand a separate electorate for Muslims.


                                              This delegation played a crucial role in the establishment of the Muslim League in 1906

                                              444

                                              In Pakistan, which is an ancient region of petroleum production?

                                              Correct Answer: Potohar Plateau
                                              Show explanation
                                              Explanation

                                              The Potwar plateau is an ancient region of petroleum production in Pakistan.

                                              The plateau is the location of Pakistan’s major oil fields, the first of which were discovered at:

                                              Khaur (1915) and Dhulian (1935)

                                              the Tut field was discovered in 1968

                                              and exploration continued in the area in the 1970s


                                              Additional information:

                                              Petroleum exploration in Pakistan began in the early 1860s during the British colonial period.


                                              The first oil well was drilled in 1866 near Kundian, Punjab, by the Burmah Oil Company. 

                                              445

                                              National Fish of Pakistan is ________?

                                              Correct Answer: Mahseer
                                              Show explanation
                                              Explanation
                                              • The National Fish of Pakistan is Mahseer
                                              • ****

                                              • Mahseer fish are found in every province throughout Pakistan in semi-cold fresh water.
                                              • It is also known as the “King of Swat”.
                                              • Pakistan is an excellent place to find Mahseer.
                                              • It is the national fish of Pakistan.

                                              • AGM 25-12-2022


                                              446

                                              Kartarpur connects Indian border with _____?

                                              Correct Answer: Dera Baba Nanak
                                              Show explanation
                                              Explanation

                                              Kartarpur connects the Indian border with Dera Baba Nanak in Punjab, India.

                                              The Kartarpur Corridor links the town of Kartarpur in Pakistan to Dera Baba Nanak in India.

                                              It facilitating visa-free travel for Sikh pilgrims.

                                              447

                                              Which Pakistani climber decided to climb Mount Everest without oxygen for a World record?

                                              Correct Answer: Mohammad Ali Saadpara
                                              Show explanation
                                              Explanation

                                              Mohammad Ali Sadpara became the first Pakistani to summit Mount Everest without supplemental oxygen on May 14, 2023.

                                              448

                                              Rafiq Tarar, the former President of Pakistan, submitted his resignation to _____?

                                              Correct Answer: General Pervez Musharraf
                                              Show explanation
                                              Explanation

                                              Rafiq Tarar, the former President of Pakistan, submitted his resignation to General Pervez Musharraf in 2001.

                                              Musharraf was the then Chief of Army Staff who had assumed power in a coup, leading to Tarar’s resignation.

                                              449

                                              Feroz Khan Noon’s government was dissolved by _______?

                                              Correct Answer: Iskandar Ali Mirza
                                              Show explanation
                                              Explanation

                                              Feroz Khan Noon’s government was dissolved by Iskandar Ali Mirza in October 1958.

                                              As President, Mirza abrogated the Constitution and dissolved the National Assembly.

                                              This led to the imposition of martial law.

                                              450

                                              The PNS Mehran base attack occurred in _______?

                                              Correct Answer: 2011
                                              Show explanation
                                              Explanation

                                              The PNS Mehran base attack occurred in 2011.

                                              On May 22, 2011, militants attacked the naval base in Karachi, resulting in the destruction of aircraft and casualties.

                                              451

                                              Pakistan said the suicide bomber who targeted the Chinese engineers was a(n) __________ national.

                                              Correct Answer: Afghan
                                              Show explanation
                                              Explanation

                                              On March 26, 2024, an Afghan suicide bomber attacked a convoy near Besham city in Khyber Pakhtunkhwa province, Pakistan.

                                              The incident resulted in the deaths of five Chinese engineers and one Pakistani driver.

                                              The convoy was traveling from Islamabad to Dasu, where a hydroelectric dam is under construction.

                                              452

                                              Length of Gwadar to Kashgar Motorway is?

                                              Correct Answer: 3000 km
                                              Show explanation
                                              Explanation

                                              This Motorway is a 3,000-km

                                              It is the network of roads, railways, and oil and gas pipelines from Gwadar port to Kashgar city in northwestern China's

                                              Gwadar is the port of origin and starting point for the CPEC.

                                              453

                                              NACTA was established under the which ministry?

                                              Correct Answer: Ministry of Interior
                                              Show explanation
                                              Explanation

                                              NACTA (National Counter Terrorism Authority NACTA Pakistan) was established in 2008.

                                              It is an administrative unit within the Ministry of Interior, led by a National Coordinator.

                                              In 2013, the NACTA Act granted it administrative and financial autonomy.

                                              It officially creating it as a body corporate answerable to a Board of Governors.

                                              454

                                              In which city is the Lok Virsa Museum located?

                                              Correct Answer: Islamabad
                                              Show explanation
                                              Explanation

                                              The Lok Virsa Museum is located in Islamabad.

                                              It is a museum of history and culture. located on the Shakarparian Hills 

                                              Heritage Museum  also known as Lok Virsa Museum

                                              The museum opened in 1974

                                              They became an autonomous institute in 2002 following the Lok Virsa Legal Status Ordinance, 2002

                                              455

                                              In 1939, on Congress Resignation, Muslim called it what day?

                                              Correct Answer: Day of Deliverance
                                              Show explanation
                                              Explanation

                                              The "Day of Deliverance"  led by Muslim League President Muhammad Ali Jinnah

                                              On 2 December 1939, Jinnah put out an appeal,

                                              calling for Indian Muslims to celebrate 22 December 1939 as a "Day of Deliverance" from Congress during the Indian independence movement.

                                              456

                                              Zhob and Kolachi rivers are located in ?

                                              Correct Answer: Balochistan
                                              Show explanation
                                              Explanation

                                              There are many rivers in Balochistan but 7 are the main

                                              The important rivers in Balochistan are:

                                              1. Zhob
                                              2. Nari
                                              3. Bolan
                                              4. Pishin
                                              5. Lora
                                              6. Mula
                                              7. Hub
                                              8. Porali
                                              9. Hingol is the largest river in the Balochistan
                                              10. Rakshan
                                              11. Dasht.

                                              ***

                                              while

                                              24 rivers are present in Pakistan

                                              5 rivers flow in Punjab Province

                                              4 rivers flow in Sindh Province

                                              7 rivers flow in Balochistan Province

                                              8 rivers flow in Khyber Pakhtunkhwa Province

                                              ND16-3-2023

                                              457

                                              Pakistan Army on launched ‘Operation Radd-ul-Fasaad’ across the country on __________?

                                              Correct Answer: 22nd Feb 2017
                                              Show explanation
                                              Explanation

                                              Operation Radd-ul-Fasaad means the Elimination of Strife جھگڑے کا خاتمہ

                                              Operation Radd ul Fasaad was started on February 22, 2017,

                                              Operation Radd ul Fasaad was started by Army Chief Qamar Javed Bajwa

                                              The foundational aim and objective of Operation Radd-ul-Fasaad is to ensure enduring peace in the country

                                              458

                                              National Highway Authority (NHA) network comprises of how many Highways and Motorways?

                                              Correct Answer: 39
                                              Show explanation
                                              Explanation

                                              The National Highway Authority (NHA) manages 39 national highways, motorways, and expressways.

                                              It totaling 12,131 kilometers in length.

                                              459

                                              Real name of Ibn-e-Insha?

                                              Correct Answer: Sher Muhammad Khan
                                              Show explanation
                                              Explanation

                                              Sher Muhammad Khan, better known by his pen name Ibn-e-Insha,

                                              He was a Pakistani Urdu poet, humorist, travelogue writer, and newspaper columnist.

                                              Born: June 15, 1927, Phillaur, India

                                              Died: January 11, 1978, London, United Kingdom

                                              Genre: Ghazal

                                              Notable awards: Pride of Performance Award in 1978 by the President of Pakistan

                                              460

                                              When was the Pakistan Protection Act established?

                                              Correct Answer: 2014
                                              Show explanation
                                              Explanation

                                              The Protection of Pakistan Act 2014 was passed by the Parliament on July 14, 2014.

                                              It is effective for two years.

                                              It aims to enhance counter-terrorism measures and prevent acts threatening national security.

                                              461

                                              Who imposed martial law in 1985?

                                              Correct Answer: Zia-ul-Haq
                                              Show explanation
                                              Explanation

                                              The Martial Law regime of General Muhamad Zia-ul-Haq.

                                              Martial Law was imposed on July 5, 1977, and officially lifted on December 30, 1985


                                              List:

                                              skandar Ali Mirza who was the first president of Pakistan abrogated the constitution of 1956 on 07-October-1958.

                                              Ø  Ayoub Khan was made chief martial law after this abrogation.

                                              Ø  Feroz Khan Noun was the prime minister at the time of the first Martial Law in Pakistan.

                                              ******

                                              List of Martial Laws Held in Pakistan from 1947 to 2022

                                              Sr#

                                               Martial Law Number

                                              Date of Martial Law

                                              Martial Law Implemented by

                                              1

                                              First Martial Law

                                              7th October 1958

                                              Iskandar Mirza

                                              2

                                              2nd Martial Law

                                              25th March 1969

                                              General Yahya Khan

                                              3

                                              3rd Martial Law

                                              25th July 1977

                                              General Zia ul Haq

                                              4

                                              4th Martial Law

                                              12th October 1999

                                              General Pervaiz Musharraf

                                               

                                              There are total four martial laws Implemented in Pakistan from 1947 to 2023

                                              Correct Answer: Khawaja Nazimuddin
                                              Show explanation
